Title: America Latina

Title: Table for Six

Title: Medusa

Title: Rewind

Title: The Chapel
Logline: A girl desperate to reach her dying mother from beyond the grave enlists a fraudulent medium to help her contact a child's spirit trapped in an old chapel for centuries.

Title: Nevermore
Logline: Two murdered girls wake in a purgatorial academy where the dead compete in lethal exams to recover the memories of how they died — and earn a second chance at life.

Title: Clinic of Horrors
Logline: In a rotting city where grotesque diseases are commonplace, a back-alley clinic cures the afflicted — for a terrible price, exacted from a young girl bound to its dark economy.

Title: Stalker x Stalker
Logline: A darkly comic horror-romance in which two obsessive stalkers unknowingly target each other, escalating into a twisted mutual courtship.

Title: Katalepsis
Logline: A mentally fragile university student who sees impossible things is pulled into a hidden world of cosmic horror and eldritch mathematics, anchored only by a slow-burn queer romance.

Title: Carousel (Will Wight-style horror serial)
Logline: Ordinary people are trapped in a malevolent town where classic horror-movie scenarios play out for real, forcing them to exploit genre tropes to survive being cast as the victims.

Title: School Bus Graveyard
Logline: After a school bus accident, a group of teens is haunted by vengeful spirits from a shadow dimension, forcing former friends and enemies to survive together.
Signal/Proof: 135M+ views; NOTE: now being adapted by WEBTOON as an animated/video-episode series (IMDb TV mini series 2026) — already in adaptation, lowers rights availability.

Title: Down to Earth (webtoon)
Logline: A charming romance between an ordinary young man and a mysterious woman from another planet, blending fish-out-of-water comedy with sci-fi warmth.

Title: Age Matters

Title: Super Secret (webtoon)

Title: Delve

Title: Purple Hyacinth

Title: #Killstagram
Logline: A young woman's viral selfie attracts a masked serial killer who begins stalking her through social media, turning her online life into a survival nightmare.

Title: Pie Game (The 8 Show)
Logline: Strangers are trapped in a deadly game of survival where a single wrong choice means death, forcing brutal psychological gamesmanship among the players.
Signal/Proof: ALREADY ADAPTED — Pie Game (sequel to Money Game) is the basis for Netflix's 'The 8 Show' (2024, Ryu Jun-yeol). NOT an available/un-optioned candidate; keep for reference/benchmark only.

Title: An Uncomfortable Truth
Logline: A seemingly gentle high schooler hides the mind of an obsessive killer, held back only by his devotion to his brother — until fate pushes him toward madness.

Title: I Love Yoo
Logline: A guarded young woman with a traumatic past gets entangled with two wealthy stepbrothers, unraveling family secrets in a slow-burn drama-mystery.

Title: Of What Remains

Title: Twig
Logline: In an alternate Victorian world of bioengineering, a squad of lab-made child operatives use manipulation and intellect as weapons in a chilling conspiracy thriller.

Title: The Gwichon Village Mystery

Creator/Author/Director: YELLOW_SHEEP (Hwang Yang), art by Daehan Lee

Title: Wait, Your Car?
Logline: The friendship of four young women is tested when one insists her car is literally trying to kill her — a supernatural comedy that plays as a sly allegory for believing a friend in crisis.
Signal/Proof: 15-min supernatural comedy short (~34K views in 11 days); writer/director Reece Feldman has a large TikTok following (guywithamoviecamera); cast includes Ruby Cruz and Whitney Peak.

Title: Unpaid Interns
Logline: Sketch comedy group.
Why It Matters: Would be curious to know what a genre movie with their sensibility would be.

Title: Adam Somang Karlson
Logline: Created a webcomic called The Public Domain where public-domain characters fight to the death; also has a script called 8 Limbs about Muay Thai fighters fighting aliens.
Why It Matters: Would be interested in learning more about both scripts; The Public Domain could be a good short.

Title: Walker Ward
Logline: Following his pivot from finance, Ward gained a cult following for deadpan character work and relatable corporate / country-club humor. Hosts a digital series called Pedicab Connections (produced by Sharp Entertainment), acting as a mobile matchmaker on the streets of NYC.
Why It Matters: Would be curious to see a short form in the world of finance or elite society — maybe a brother-president gets kidnapped and he needs to pretend to be him so the world doesn't collapse.
Action Items: Emailed 6/26. Met with his rep Fran — Walker is busy making a show but referred another client, Sam Swank; sent pilot 'Big Alcohol' (possible feature), liked but didn't love it.

Title: Sam Swank
Logline: Creator referred by Walker Ward. Has a script called 'Big Alcohol' he's pitching.
Why It Matters: Didn't love 'Big Alcohol' but his videos are good; the one based on a son and father-in-law could be funny.

Title: RDCworld1
Logline: YouTube comedy channel making skits, vlogs, short films and more with a large, engaged fanbase.

Title: Hughhocknoll
Logline: Creator making comedy and action content from shorts to full feature films; does his own stunts. Invites collaboration from concept to premiere.
Why It Matters: Specialized in comedy and action, does stunts. Could be good to pair with the Mayhem Bros.

Title: Grant Beans
Action Items: Talked with manager, waiting on General.

Title: Michael Kandel
Logline: Made a 'The Pitt' video game that went viral.

Title: Chris & Jack
Logline: Chris W. Smith and Jack De Sena release comedic sketches of all kinds — sci-fi, heists, animation, movie tropes, absurdist bits — with friendship at the core.
Creator/Author/Director: Chris W. Smith & Jack De Sena

Title: Syd & Olivia
Logline: Comedy duo who write, produce, direct, edit and star in original sketch videos — fast-paced, joke-heavy dialogue subverting gender stereotypes with absurdist humor. Currently developing a live-action comedy with 20th Century Studios for Hulu (Megan Ganz' Megawatt Productions). Their short 'In Jeff We Trust' premiered at Tribeca 2025. Credits: The Paloni Show! (Hulu), work for Adam McKay's Yellowdot, SMOSH; podcast 'Syd & Olivia Talk S**T' (7EQUIS).
Action Items: Made a short called In Jeff We Trust (Tribeca 2025).

Title: Copycat
Logline: A washed-up art instructor becomes obsessed with a serial killer who stages victims as if murdered by their own doubles — and the deeper he investigates, the more the killings implicate him.
Why It Matters: From the Sweet Home / Bastard team (Carnby Kim), whose work has a proven Netflix adaptation track record. Grounded serial-killer horror with a high-concept hook — squarely Hammer's lane. Debuted Apr 2026; this team's IP gets optioned fast, so move early.

Title: Vincent the Great
Logline: An excommunicated priest hunts the demons picking off the world's exorcists, aided by a nun, a sheriff, and a child who bleeds rose petals and kills anything that bites her.
Why It Matters: "Constantine meets Hellsing" gothic-Americana exorcism horror — a rare US-set WEBTOON with strong feature/limited-series bones and (as a brand-new launch) likely available rights.

Title: Destined Killer
Logline: A vigilante serial killer who can see how many people each stranger is fated to murder meets a woman who sees how long anyone has left to live — and her numbers start to change.
Why It Matters: Twist-driven serial-killer premise with a clean two-hander engine and a built-in moral hook. Contained and castable — a natural film or limited series.

Title: The Tale of the Skeleton Messenger
Logline: Cursed with immortality by the dying Demon Lord, a warrior withers into an undying skeleton and returns centuries later as the herald of a corrupt empire's reckoning.
Why It Matters: Bleak, Frieren-adjacent immortality fable with a striking visual identity; one of the most-talked-about new manhwa of early 2026.

Title: The Frostbound Warrior
Logline: Goryeo's greatest swordsman wakes after centuries frozen in ice, bent on revenge — then becomes bodyguard-manager to his lost love's descendant, a rising actress.
Why It Matters: Inverts the regression trope — a man out of time thrust into modern showbiz. Fish-out-of-water hook with a built-in contemporary setting makes it unusually adaptable for the genre.

Title: Return of the Demonic Instructor
Logline: A powerless martial artist, brainwashed into commanding a demon army during an alien-demon invasion, regresses ten years to stop the apocalypse before it begins.
Why It Matters: Murim-meets-sci-fi-apocalypse regression; genre-blending hook, though dense mythology and heavy world-building lower feature adaptability.

Title: Ghost Dating Simulation
Logline: A streamer is trapped inside a dating sim made real, where he must romance dangerous ghosts to survive — every wrong choice is lethal, and he has a limited number of lives.
Why It Matters: High-concept horror-comedy with a killer logline that whiplashes between terrifying and funny — a natural genre-film pitch, and rights are likely open.

Title: Zodiac Girls
Logline: A young man becomes referee for a supernatural rematch of the Chinese zodiac race when the people around him start becoming vessels for the twelve zodiac spirits.
Why It Matters: "Fruits Basket via the zodiac race" — a clean mythic hook with ensemble and animation potential.

Title: Superfish
Logline: A skeptic accidentally bonds to a witch's magical core on a night out, becoming a magnet for supernatural predators — with the reluctant witch forced to protect him.
Why It Matters: Forced-proximity urban fantasy from an established creator (To the Stars and Back). Buzzy 2025 launch with a strong, stylish voice.

Title: The Bathroom Ghost
Logline: A ghost girl in training haunts a shopping-mall bathroom and keeps failing to scare anyone, navigating an afterlife of supernatural bureaucracy.
Why It Matters: Charming Canvas-to-Original success; light supernatural-comedy IP with animation appeal and open rights.

Title: Miss Pendleton
Logline: A celebrated Regency matchmaker, oblivious to romance when she's the target, is hired to find a match for a brooding recluse who is quietly falling for her instead.
Why It Matters: "Bridgerton meets Pride & Prejudice" with no fantasy elements — an easy period-romance adaptation comp.

Title: My Bias Gets on the Last Train
Logline: A fan keeps meeting a mysterious woman on the late-night train who shares his love of an anonymous indie band — not knowing she's secretly its lead singer.
Why It Matters: Idol / fan-culture romance (Oshi no Ko adjacent) with breakout art and 26M+ views; a strong K-drama comp.

Title: There's No Such Thing as a Bad Lady
Logline: A slum-raised mage is hired to tutor a group of noblewomen branded "bad ladies" by society, in a world where magic is status and rumor is a weapon.
Creator/Author/Director: Korita; adapted by Shin Heebin; art by TEAM PINKSHARK
Why It Matters: Class-and-rumor social fantasy with sharp dialogue; a quiet standout among 2026 romance-fantasy debuts.

Title: I Will Become the Villain's Poison Taster
Logline: Reincarnated as a death-row villainess who is immune to poison and can identify any toxin by taste, a woman talks her way into the villains' service as their poison taster to rewrite her fate.
Why It Matters: "Apothecary Diaries"-adjacent otome-isekai with a fresh detection hook.

Title: Fae Trapped
Logline: A fae wrongly branded a traitor and exiled from her homeland accidentally swaps bodies with a tortured human sculptor when a spell to return home goes wrong.
Why It Matters: From instantmiso (Siren's Lament) — proven fandom, lush art, and a body-swap romance hook.

Title: Curveball and Splitter
Logline: A burned-out office worker who jokes about hiring a hitman for his bosses is upended by the return of a former high-school baseball teammate nursing a long-held grudge.
Why It Matters: Acclaimed BL sports drama; a character study of addiction and self-destruction. Manta exclusive.

Title: Dark Guardian
Logline: Lucifer calls off a wager with God over an orphan boy's soul when the boy sees through his deception, and the two agree to meet once a year until the day his soul is claimed.
Why It Matters: Tight 10-chapter theological BL about salvation and eternity; a contained, striking premise.

Title: The Keluarga Cable Ship Company
Logline: A father and son who can't see eye to eye end up on the same cable-repair ship maintaining the internet's undersea infrastructure, their diverging stories told in three parallel scrolling columns.
Why It Matters: Formally daring family drama told in three parallel scrolling columns; a distinctive indie voice with wide-open rights.

Title: trAPPed
Logline: An Indian woman is ensnared by a "digital arrest" phone scam — con artists posing as police — in a horror story that weaponizes the infinite scroll and the cold glow of the screen.
Creator/Author/Director: Suparna Sharma, Natalie Obiko Pearson & Anand RK
Why It Matters: Ripped-from-reality tech-horror published by Bloomberg; timely, contained, and cinematic — a real-world horror premise with festival / limited-series potential.

Title: Tiger Girls
Logline: In a society where girls born in the Year of the Tiger are killed as bad omens, the survivors hide on remote islands and train for the mainland's next assault.
Why It Matters: Singaporean feminist dystopia with a razor-sharp premise and a strong world; a fresh non-Korean voice with rights wide open.

Title: Flying Saucer Video
Logline: The messy, unabashedly queer staff of a closing video-rental store navigate friendship and romance in the final days before everything changes.
Why It Matters: Nostalgic, character-driven queer ensemble dramedy; strong indie voice and open rights. (18+)

Title: Foreach
Logline: Four kids escape the pressures of their lives through video games in four different genres — until the borders between their worlds start to fracture and bleed together.
Why It Matters: Formally inventive webcomic that fuses four game genres with narrative; a distinctive concept with open rights.

Title: Time Will Devour His Children
Logline: Two transmasculine sorcerers, trained under a now-rogue master in a rigidly gender-segregated order, set out to find him — and confront what they were made to become.
Why It Matters: Literary, uncompromising fantasy on gender and respectability from an award-caliber cartoonist; prestige indie IP.

Title: Shall I Write Your Love Letter?
Logline: A ghostwriter transported into a bleak BL novel as a doomed minor villain must avoid writing the very love letters that get him killed by the story's leading man.
Why It Matters: Meta otome-isekai flipped to BL; a playful survival-of-the-narrative hook. English webtoon adaptation of a 2022 novel; Lezhin exclusive (18+).

Title: The Prince Doesn't Cry Over Onions
Logline: A kind village restaurateur unknowingly puts a war-cursed "tyrant prince" — who can't feel emotion but is oddly great at peeling onions — to work in her kitchen.
Why It Matters: Warm, comedic historical-fantasy romance; a breakout Tapas debut with a charming hook.

Title: MiSide
Logline: A player is sucked inside a cute mobile dating sim and trapped by its sweet-then-sinister AI girlfriend, who won't let him log out.
Why It Matters: One of 2024's breakout horror hits — a meta yandere premise that translates cleanly to screen as tech-horror. Big streaming footprint; rights likely already courted, so it's a watch-and-move-fast title.

Title: Look Outside
Logline: Told not to look outside during an apocalyptic event, a tenant must survive a building full of grotesquely mutated neighbors in a turn-based horror RPG.
Why It Matters: A 2025 critical darling built on psychological dread over jumpscares. Contained single-building setting and a strong central rule ('don't look') make it a tight, filmable concept with open rights.

Title: That's Not My Neighbor
Logline: A 1950s apartment doorman must spot doppelgangers impersonating residents before he lets a monster inside.
Why It Matters: A viral 'Papers, Please meets body-snatchers' hook with a strong period aesthetic. Simple, high-concept premise that reads instantly — easy to pitch as contained paranoia horror.

Title: Sorry We're Closed
Logline: A heartbroken woman cursed to see demons must navigate love and survival when a powerful demon falls for her, in a Resident Evil-style survival horror.
Why It Matters: Acclaimed, stylish, and character-driven queer survival horror with a distinctive cast and world — the kind of authored voice that adapts well. Small studio, rights likely available.

Title: Hollowbody
Logline: In a near-future Britain, a woman ventures into a quarantined tech-ruined zone to find someone she lost, uncovering a haunted, decaying world.
Why It Matters: A solo-dev Silent Hill successor with strong atmosphere and a sci-fi-tinged British setting. Distinctive tone and a self-contained story; solo creator means clean rights.

Title: Bye Sweet Carole
Logline: A young woman investigates the disappearance of a missing girl and is pulled into a dark, hand-drawn fairy-tale world of monsters and secrets.
Why It Matters: Hand-drawn in the style of golden-age Disney animation but sinister — a visually singular property with obvious animated-feature potential from the creator of Remothered.

Title: The Midnight Walk
Logline: A burnt traveler and a small light-giving creature journey through a handcrafted world of darkness and fire in a claymation dark fable.
Why It Matters: Entirely handcrafted from clay — a gorgeous, tactile art direction that would translate to stop-motion animation. Distinctive authored world with strong festival/animation appeal.

Title: It Has My Face
Logline: Someone discovers their own face is being worn by something else, and must hunt down the impostor wearing their identity to survive.
Why It Matters: A literal identity-theft horror hook — doppelganger paranoia distilled to its purest, most cinematic form. Brand-new (2026), pixel-art indie with wide-open rights.

Title: House of Necrosis
Logline: A survivor explores a decaying mansion overrun by grotesque infection, in a fixed-camera, tank-controls throwback to PS1-era survival horror.
Why It Matters: A loving Spencer Mansion homage — classic haunted-house survival horror bones. More of a genre comp/mood reference than a standout premise, but a clean rights target.

Title: Heartworm
Logline: Grieving the loss of loved ones, a woman armed with a camera enters a surreal, haunted house said to let the living contact the dead.
Why It Matters: Fatal Frame-style camera combat with a grief-driven, dreamlike story — an emotionally grounded horror premise with strong visual identity and a long-anticipated fanbase.

Title: Post Trauma
Logline: A middle-aged train conductor wakes in a nightmarish parallel world and must survive its monstrous horrors to find a way home.
Why It Matters: A classic fixed-camera survival horror in the Silent Hill mold with an atypical everyman protagonist. Solid genre execution; useful as a tonal comp and a rights target.

Title: Amanda the Adventurer 2
Logline: Watching a cheerful lost children's cartoon on old VHS tapes, a viewer realizes the show and its host are aware of them — and hiding something monstrous.
Why It Matters: The sequel in a hugely popular analog-horror franchise with deep streaming/YouTube reach. Creepy-kids'-show conceit is very adaptable; established brand with a built-in audience.

Title: Kletka
Logline: Trapped in a slow descending Soviet-era elevator, a small group must survive the creatures that climb in with them the deeper they go.
Why It Matters: A viral co-op creature-horror with a brutally simple contained premise (one elevator, going down). Reads as a lean single-location horror — easy to pitch, brand-new, open rights.

Title: Karma: The Dark World
Logline: In a totalitarian alternate 1984, a state agent dives into suspects' memories to root out dissent, and starts losing his grip on what's real.
Why It Matters: A cinematic, Kafkaesque dystopian horror with a bold visual style — strong world and imagery that would translate to a stylish genre feature. Small studio; rights likely available.

Title: Tormented Souls 2
Logline: A woman searching for her sister confronts body-horror nightmares and puzzles in a remote, cursed village steeped in ritual.
Why It Matters: A well-crafted sequel keeping fixed-camera survival horror alive. More of a genre comp than a distinct pitch, but a clean rights target from an indie team.

Title: The Bathhouse
Logline: A man visits an old public bathhouse to confront a debt and becomes trapped inside with a deformed, murderous presence.
Why It Matters: A PS1-styled Japanese/Russian body-horror set in a bathhouse — a strong single-location premise with grimy atmosphere. Niche but distinctive; open rights.

Title: Dead Take
Logline: An actor searches a lavish mansion for a missing friend after a party, piecing together disturbing footage in a live-action horror mystery about Hollywood.
Why It Matters: A live-action FMV psychological horror set in the film industry, with genre-star talent — the FMV format and Hollywood-insider setting make it unusually screen-ready.

Title: Blood Typers
Logline: Survivors fight off a monster outbreak across a city entirely by typing words, in a retro survival-horror homage.
Why It Matters: A clever typing-based survival horror with a co-op twist — the gimmick is the appeal. Fun genre curio; lower feature adaptability, but wide-open rights.

Title: Cronos: The New Dawn
Logline: A time-traveling agent returns to 1980s Poland to extract the souls of the doomed before an apocalyptic event turns humans into monsters.
Why It Matters: An original sci-fi body-horror IP with a striking time-travel hook. Bloober is a larger studio, so rights are more complex — track as a strong genre comp and IP to monitor.

Title: Slitterhead
Logline: A bodiless entity possesses humans across a neon-lit city to hunt shape-shifting monsters disguised as people.
Why It Matters: From the creator of Silent Hill and Siren — a body-swap creature-horror with a bold hook. Pedigree makes it notable; larger studio, so rights are more complex.

Title: Conscript
Logline: A French soldier searches the hellish WWI battlefields of Verdun for his missing brother, surviving both the enemy and the horrors of war.
Why It Matters: Survival horror set in the trenches of WWI — a genuinely fresh, cinematic setting for the genre. Distinctive premise and a solo/small team; strong rights opportunity.

Title: Zoochosis
Logline: A night-shift zookeeper discovers the animals are turning into monsters and must diagnose and cure them before the infection spreads to humans.
Why It Matters: A body-horror zookeeper sim with a gross, memorable hook that blew up on streaming. Distinctive premise; small studio with open rights.

Title: Strange Antiquities
Logline: The keeper of a shop of cursed occult objects identifies and controls dark artifacts for a town full of strange, needy customers.
Why It Matters: An atmospheric occult-shop sim (follow-up to Strange Horticulture) — rich, moody worldbuilding more than a plot engine. Better as tone/aesthetic reference; open rights.

Title: Sulfur
Logline: A pilgrim armed with scavenged guns ventures into a strange, hostile wilderness ruled by a mad god in a roguelite shooter.
Why It Matters: A grimy roguelite FPS with horror atmosphere and a cult following — more a vibe/world reference than a narrative pitch. Low feature adaptability; open rights.

Title: The Lacerator
Logline: A cocky 1980s adult-film star is trapped in a sadistic killer's lair and must fight his way out, adapting as he loses limbs. (18+)
Why It Matters: An 80s-set slasher survival horror leaning into gore and satire, with a dynamic dismemberment hook. Pulpy and very genre; adult content, but a clear tonal swing and open rights.

Title: Sébastien Vaniček
Logline: French genre director who broke out with the acclaimed creature feature Infested; now helming Evil Dead Burn (2026).
Why It Matters: Rising fast — went from festival shorts to a Fantastic Fest-winning debut to a studio Evil Dead film in three years. Proven creature-horror craft on a budget; track him before he's fully locked into franchise work. (Requested: Infested director.)

Title: Harry Tordjman
Logline: French producer at My Box Films; produced Sébastien Vaniček's breakout creature feature Infested.
Why It Matters: Producing partner behind one of the most successful recent French genre debuts. A useful French-side producer contact for co-productions and emerging-talent packaging. (Requested: Infested producer.)

Title: Just Philippot
Logline: French director of eco-horror The Swarm (La Nuée) and the apocalyptic thriller Acide.
Why It Matters: Grounded, character-first genre filmmaker who turns ecological dread into contained horror. Two well-received features; not yet locked to a studio.

Title: Ludovic & Zoran Boukherma
Logline: French twin-brother directors behind the werewolf tale Teddy, shark-comedy The Year of the Shark, and Leurs enfants après eux.
Why It Matters: Prolific young genre voices comfortable blending horror, comedy, and social realism. A directing duo with range and a growing festival reputation.

Title: Thomas Cailley
Logline: French director of the acclaimed creature/mutation drama The Animal Kingdom (Le Règne animal).
Why It Matters: Brings prestige-drama craft to high-concept genre — The Animal Kingdom paired creature effects with real emotion and heavy César attention. Elevated genre with crossover appeal.
Source Link: The Animal Kingdom / Le Règne animal (2023)

Title: Prano Bailey-Bond
Logline: British director whose debut Censor, set in the video-nasty era, was a Sundance-launched critical hit.
Why It Matters: Distinct voice in elevated British horror with deep genre literacy. One feature in — still very much a gettable, festival-tier director.

Title: Rose Glass
Logline: British writer-director of the religious-horror debut Saint Maud and the noir thriller Love Lies Bleeding.
Why It Matters: A24-caliber genre stylist rising fast but not yet studio-locked. Saint Maud is exactly the kind of contained, character-driven horror Hammer favors.

Title: Rob Savage
Logline: British horror director who broke out with the lockdown hit Host, then Dashcam and the studio film The Boogeyman.
Why It Matters: Fast, inventive, very-online horror sensibility with proven ability to scale from micro-budget to studio. Strong for contained, high-concept scares.

Title: Matt Palmer
Logline: British director of the taut Netflix thriller Calibre; recently directed Fear Street: Prom Queen.
Why It Matters: Excellent at slow-burn moral-dread thrillers. Has moved into studio horror but still an emerging, hireable genre director.

Title: Charlotte Colbert
Logline: British artist-filmmaker whose debut She Will was a stylish, witch-tinged psychological horror.
Why It Matters: Strong visual/art-world sensibility applied to genre. One feature in; a distinctive voice for atmospheric, female-led horror.

Title: Louis Paxton
Logline: Scottish writer-director of award-winning horror and comedy shorts; feature debut The Incomer premiered at Sundance.
Why It Matters: Comedy-horror voice with a folklore-tinged Scottish-island debut. Very early-stage — exactly the pre-breakout profile worth locking early.

Title: Indianna Bell & Josiah Allen
Logline: Australian directing duo behind the claustrophobic single-location thriller You'll Never Find Me.
Why It Matters: Made a tense, contained, Shudder-acquired debut on clearly modest means — an ideal profile for a low-budget genre feature.

Title: Matt Vesely
Logline: Australian director of the single-location sci-fi thriller Monolith.
Why It Matters: One-actor, one-house sci-fi debut that punched well above its budget. Strong contained-thriller instincts; emerging and gettable.

Title: Hannah Barlow & Kane Senes
Logline: Australian duo behind the social-media slasher-comedy Sissy.
Why It Matters: Sharp, internet-native horror-comedy voice. A duo who treat influencer culture as horror fuel — very current and marketable.

Title: Chris Broadbent
Logline: Tasmanian Indigenous writer-director; debut It Will Find You brings a First Nations lens to supernatural horror.
Why It Matters: Fresh cultural perspective on the genre with a debut building festival momentum (SXSW Sydney). Pre-wide-release — an early-mover opportunity.

Title: David Vincent Smith
Logline: Australian filmmaker whose festival short I'm Not Hurting You is being expanded into a feature.
Why It Matters: A prodigious short-film talent making the jump to features (an addiction/kidnap thriller). Worth tracking at the shorts-to-feature inflection point.

Title: Carlota Pereda
Logline: Spanish writer-director of the acclaimed rural body-image slasher Piggy (Cerdita) and follow-up The Chapel.
Why It Matters: Genre with a social edge; a Goya-winning short expanded into a festival-hit feature. Distinct, marketable voice in Spanish horror.

Title: Caye Casas
Logline: Spanish director of the pitch-black single-apartment horror-comedy The Coffee Table.
Why It Matters: Master of minimalist, excruciating dread-comedy built from one terrible mistake. Ultra-contained premises that are cheap to make and unforgettable.
Source Link: The Coffee Table / La mesita del comedor (2022)

Title: Paul Urkijo Alijo
Logline: Basque writer-director of folk-horror fantasies Errementari and Irati, steeped in regional mythology.
Why It Matters: Builds richly designed dark-fairy-tale worlds on independent budgets. Distinctive folklore-horror sensibility with real world-building chops.

Title: David Casademunt
Logline: Spanish director whose debut The Wasteland (El páramo) was an atmospheric period psychological horror for Netflix.
Why It Matters: Restrained, mood-driven horror craft; a single-family, single-location debut. Emerging and clearly capable of contained genre.

Title: Alberto Corredor
Logline: Spanish director whose feature debut Baghead expanded his own short into a shape-shifting supernatural horror.
Why It Matters: Short-to-feature genre director with a clean high-concept monster hook. Early-career and gettable.

Title: Michiel Blanchart
Logline: Belgian writer-director of the single-night action-thriller Night Call; his Oscar-shortlisted short is being remade by Sam Raimi.
Why It Matters: Swept the Magritte Awards with an assured, propulsive debut and already has Hollywood attention via the Raimi-produced remake. High-ceiling action/thriller director to engage before he's fully in the US studio system. (Requested.)
Source Link: Night Call / La nuit se traîne (2024)

Title: Jean-Christophe Reymond
Logline: French producer, founder of Kazak Productions; lead producer on Julia Ducournau's Palme d'Or winner Titane.
Why It Matters: Champions radical auteur genre on modest budgets; a proven French partner for elevated body-horror and boundary-pushing material.

Title: Amaury Ovise
Logline: Producer at Kazak Productions, developing genre and auteur voices from short films to features.
Why It Matters: Reymond's producing partner and a route into Kazak's pipeline of emerging French genre talent.

Title: Manuel Chiche
Logline: Founder of The Jokers, France's leading genre film label as both distributor and producer.
Why It Matters: The tastemaker behind French releases of much elevated horror. A key contact for the French genre market and co-productions.

Title: Jean des Forêts
Logline: French producer at Petit Film; backed Julia Ducournau's cannibal-coming-of-age debut Raw (Grave).
Why It Matters: A producer with a taste for transgressive debuts; Petit Film has a track record launching bold first-time genre directors.

Title: Margaux Marciano
Logline: French producer at Quad; co-produced Michiel Blanchart's action-thriller Night Call.
Why It Matters: Bridges France's commercial machine (Quad) with emerging genre — a co-production partner tied to the Night Call team.

Title: Michaël Goldberg
Logline: Belgian producer at Boucan Productions; lead producer of Michiel Blanchart's Night Call.
Why It Matters: Shepherded a 10-Magritte-winning debut. A Belgian genre partner with proven festival-to-market results. (Night Call producer.)

Title: Boris Van Gils
Logline: Belgian producer at Formosa Productions; co-producer of Michiel Blanchart's Night Call.
Why It Matters: Formosa co-produced one of Belgium's biggest recent genre successes — a useful Benelux production ally.

Title: Oliver Kassman
Logline: British producer, founder of Escape Plan; produced Rose Glass's Saint Maud and Love Lies Bleeding.
Why It Matters: One of the UK's sharpest elevated-genre producers, a decade backing singular horror voices. Squarely the Hammer wheelhouse.

Title: Andrea Cornwell
Logline: British producer at Lobo Films; co-produced Saint Maud and Love Lies Bleeding.
Why It Matters: Kassman's partner on two of the UK's most acclaimed recent genre films; a reliable British co-production lead.

Title: Andy Starke
Logline: British producer, founder of Rook Films; produced Possessor, In Fabric and Sightseers.
Why It Matters: The go-to producer for Wheatley- and Strickland-style transgressive British genre. Cult sensibility with real festival pedigree.

Title: Matthew James Wilkinson
Logline: British producer, founder of Stigma Films; produced Romola Garai's horror Amulet.
Why It Matters: Prolific young UK producer comfortable across horror, comedy and drama; builds first features for new directors.

Title: Jed Shepherd
Logline: British writer-producer behind the lockdown-horror hits Host and Dashcam with Rob Savage.
Why It Matters: Fast, format-inventive, very-online horror producer. Ideal for high-concept, low-cost scares with viral potential.

Title: Helen Jones
Logline: British producer at Silver Salt Films; produced Prano Bailey-Bond's Censor.
Why It Matters: Backed one of the UK's most distinctive recent horror debuts; aligned with elevated, festival-tier genre.

Title: Jennifer Handorf
Logline: British genre producer behind Prevenge, The Borderlands and Possum.
Why It Matters: A dedicated UK horror producer with a long indie-genre track record; deep genre-community ties and micro-budget craft.

Title: Andy Brunskill
Logline: British producer at SUMS Film & Media; developed the psychological chiller The Visitor.
Why It Matters: Emerging UK producer building a slate of dark, contained genre. An early-stage relationship worth forming.

Title: Kristina Ceyton
Logline: Australian producer, co-founder of Causeway Films; produced The Babadook, Talk to Me and Bring Her Back.
Why It Matters: One of the most successful genre producers working — a repeat A24 partner who turns bold Aussie horror into global hits. Top-tier.

Title: Samantha Jennings
Logline: Australian producer, co-founder of Causeway Films; produced Talk to Me and Bring Her Back.
Why It Matters: Ceyton's partner and the on-set producer the Philippous credit for shaping Talk to Me. Elite genre packaging and talent development.

Title: Daniel Negret
Logline: Producer and Causeway Films' UK-based CEO; ex-Head Gear financier on Talk to Me.
Why It Matters: Bridges creative and finance for international genre; a route to Causeway's slate and co-financing structures.

Title: Adrián Guerra
Logline: Spanish producer, co-founder of Nostromo Pictures; produced Buried and Bird Box Barcelona.
Why It Matters: Specialist in contained, high-concept genre with international reach; a proven Spanish partner for English-language horror-thrillers.

Title: Núria Valls
Logline: Spanish producer at Nostromo Pictures; produced Bird Box Barcelona.
Why It Matters: Guerra's producing partner; Nostromo's genre slate travels internationally — a strong Spanish co-production contact.

Title: Enrique López Lavigne
Logline: Veteran Spanish producer at Apache Films; produced 28 Weeks Later, Verónica and The Impossible.
Why It Matters: A lifelong horror fan with 30 years of genre and prestige credits; deep resources and relationships across Spanish and international film.

Title: Carlos Juárez
Logline: Basque producer at Basque Films; produced the global hit The Platform (El Hoyo) and its sequel.
Why It Matters: Turned a contained dystopian-horror concept into a Netflix phenomenon. Expert at high-concept genre on efficient budgets.

Title: Álvaro Longoria
Logline: Spanish producer, founder of Morena Films; produced Carlota Pereda's Piggy.
Why It Matters: Backs socially-charged genre with festival pedigree; ties directly to Pereda, from a substantial Spanish production house.

Title: Belén Atienza
Logline: Spanish producer at Apache Films; produced A Monster Calls and The Impossible.
Why It Matters: Prestige-scale producer comfortable with dark fantasy and survival material; access to larger budgets and effects-driven genre.

Title: Sandra Hermida
Logline: Spanish producer behind The Orphanage and Society of the Snow.
Why It Matters: Producer with supernatural and survival pedigree operating at an international, awards-tier scale.
Source Link: The Orphanage (2007) / Society of the Snow (2023)

Title: The Glendale Archives
Logline: Analog-horror YouTube series framed as recovered archival tapes: an archivist shares footage of a man seemingly alone on Earth, surrounded by creatures he calls “nesters.”
Why It Matters: Backrooms-adjacent but with a stronger built-in narrative — a lonely-survivor mythology plus original creature (“nester”) design that could anchor a contained, atmospheric horror feature. Early, ownable internet IP with a distinctive POV.
Source Link: YouTube — The Glendale Archives (also on Instagram)
Action Items: Talked with creators. They're working on a feature. Will send us a take when ready

Title: Denver International Airport Conspiracy
Logline: The internet's favorite airport conspiracy — apocalyptic murals, a demonic blue horse that killed its sculptor, and rumored underground bunkers for the elite.
Why It Matters: Massive, evergreen public conspiracy with built-in iconography (Blucifer, the doomsday murals) and no rights to clear — a studio can build conspiracy-horror or dark comedy around it freely. The template for this whole lane.

Title: Cicada 3301
Logline: A shadowy organization posts the internet's hardest cryptographic puzzle to recruit codebreakers worldwide — and no one knows who is behind it or why.
Why It Matters: Ready-made recruitment-thriller premise (Fincher/Nolan register) with a real, unsolved mystery and a devoted puzzle-solving community. Public events, no single rights holder.

Title: The Max Headroom Incident
Logline: In 1987 a masked hijacker seized two Chicago TV signals for ninety seconds of surreal menace — and was never identified.
Why It Matters: An unsolved real event with an indelible image and a large online sleuth community. A perfect period thriller/horror seed, and completely unowned.

Title: Polybius
Logline: An urban legend about a 1981 arcade game that caused seizures, hallucinations and disappearances — allegedly a government mind-control experiment.
Why It Matters: 80s-set techno-paranoia with a cult following and no owner. High-concept period-genre catnip that has never had a definitive film.

Title: The Mandela Catalogue
Logline: Analog-horror series where doppelganger 'Alternates' — one disguised as the archangel Gabriel — psychologically torture a rural county into replacing humanity.
Why It Matters: A crown jewel of analog horror with a huge theory-driven fanbase and a genuinely cinematic religious-horror mythology. Note: creator Alex Kister has said a feature film is in the works — track availability closely.

Title: Gemini Home Entertainment
Logline: Fake home-video tapes chronicle an extraterrestrial invasion by a sentient planet, 'The Iris,' and a parasitic 'Deep Root Disease' that turns people into woodcrawlers.
Why It Matters: Cosmic and body horror with a strong creature mythology and dedicated fandom. Single-creator IP that could option cleanly; squarely the Hammer wheelhouse.

Title: Local 58
Logline: A public-access channel is hijacked across decades by broadcasts urging viewers to look at the moon — the series that named the analog-horror genre.
Why It Matters: The genre-defining analog-horror work, by an identifiable creator (Kris Straub, also behind 'Candle Cove'). Contained, atmospheric and eminently adaptable.

Title: Petscop
Logline: A let's-play of a lost, unfinished PS1 pet-catching game curdles into a coded confession about child abuse and 'rebirthing' therapy.
Why It Matters: A beloved, endlessly-analyzed mystery box with a devoted community and a devastating real-world thematic core (the Candace Newmaker case). Single-author and unadapted.

Title: Ben Drowned
Logline: A college student's secondhand copy of Majora's Mask is haunted by BEN, a malevolent AI ghost of a drowned boy.
Why It Matters: Foundational haunted-media creepypasta with a millions-strong fandom and a clear single author. Obvious cinematic hooks around cursed technology.

Title: The Dionaea House
Logline: Through emails and blogs, friends uncover a house that devours people and expands across space and time.
Why It Matters: Epistolary haunted-house classic written by now A-list screenwriter Eric Heisserer (Arrival, Bird Box). Note: optioned to Warner Bros. back in 2005 — worth checking whether rights have reverted.

Title: Siren Head (Trevor Henderson)
Logline: A towering, siren-headed creature that broadcasts distorted sounds to lure prey — the flagship of Trevor Henderson's viral monster universe.
Why It Matters: A merchandising-ready monster with a massive fan community and a named artist to option. A pure creature-feature engine that has not yet had a major film.

Title: NES Godzilla Creepypasta
Logline: A hacked Godzilla NES cartridge births an original monster, 'Red,' who stalks the player through corrupted, impossible levels.
Why It Matters: An epic, image-rich haunted-game saga with a cult following and an original monster IP ('Red'). Largely un-mined by Hollywood.

Title: Lake City Quiet Pills
Logline: A cryptic imgur account and coded messages hint at a ring of retired mercenaries — a real internet mystery that went dark before it could be solved.
Why It Matters: A grounded le-Carre-meets-Reddit conspiracy with an obsessive sleuth community and no owner. Strong seed for a rights-free thriller.

Title: Missing 411
Logline: A retired detective catalogs eerily patterned disappearances in national parks that officials can't explain.
Why It Matters: Huge community and a proven nonfiction franchise — a natural for elevated wilderness horror. Note: rights sit with author David Paulides; track availability.

Title: Skinwalker Ranch
Logline: A Utah ranch plagued by UFOs, cattle mutilations and shapeshifting entities becomes ground zero for a paranormal investigation.
Why It Matters: Enormous crossover audience via the History series. Note: heavily rights-encumbered (Fugal / History), so more a tonal and community reference than an easy option.

Title: The Georgia Guidestones
Logline: A mysterious granite monument carved with post-apocalyptic 'commandments,' commissioned under a pseudonym — bombed and demolished in 2022.
Why It Matters: America's Stonehenge with a doomsday-cult hook and a real 2022 bombing for a built-in third act. Public-domain mystery, ripe for conspiracy horror.

Title: Ever Dream This Man? (This Man)
Logline: A website claims thousands of strangers worldwide dream of the same unremarkable man's face — who is he, and what does he want?
Why It Matters: A globally recognizable creepy-image meme with a built-in dread hook. (Origin was later tied to a marketing artist; treat the phenomenon, not the man, as the IP.)

Title: Numbers Stations
Logline: Unexplained shortwave stations broadcast strings of numbers to no one — decades-old espionage tools that still transmit today.
Why It Matters: Atmospheric, paranoid and real, with a devoted listener community. Unowned; a strong spy-horror or contained-thriller device.

Title: The Hum
Logline: A low-frequency hum only some people can hear drives a town to the edge — and no one can find the source.
Why It Matters: A real, unexplained sensory mystery with global sufferer communities. A perfect small-town paranoia / sound-horror premise; rights-free.

Title: The Bloop
Logline: In 1997 hydrophones captured an ultra-low ocean sound louder than any known animal — nicknamed 'The Bloop.'
Why It Matters: A ready-made deep-sea creature hook with lingering online mystique (despite the later icequake explanation). Public-domain; a classic monster-movie seed.

Title: The Black-Eyed Children
Logline: Pale children with solid-black eyes appear at doors and car windows, calmly begging to be let in.
Why It Matters: A simple, terrifying, instantly-filmable premise with widespread folklore currency and no owner. Pure contained-horror fuel.

Title: Fresno Nightcrawlers
Logline: Two spindly, pants-shaped creatures that walk on stilt-like legs, caught on a Fresno security camera and beloved online.
Why It Matters: A weirdly endearing cryptid with meme ubiquity and a real fandom — strong for a Gremlins-style creature comedy-horror. Unowned folklore.

Title: Ted the Caver
Logline: One of the web's first horror stories: a blog documents two friends squeezing deeper into a cave toward something that does not want them there.
Why It Matters: Proto-internet horror with a devoted following and a brutally cinematic single-location claustrophobia engine (very 'The Descent'). Effectively unowned.

Title: This House Has People In It
Logline: A family's surveillance footage, watched through a bizarre monitoring 'portal,' slowly reveals something deeply wrong inside the home.
Why It Matters: A cult surreal-horror short with a named auteur (Alan Resnick) and a devoted fanbase. A distinct POV device that could expand to a feature.

Title: The Sun Vanished
Logline: A Twitter account documents, in real time, the day the sun simply disappears — and what starts coming out in the permanent dark.
Why It Matters: A real-time, format-native apocalypse with a large participatory community. High-concept survival-horror with a killer one-line hook.

Title: Alex Alert
Logline: Alex O'Hara is struggling through a divorce when everyone in Los Angeles County starts receiving emergency alerts revealing the details of his depressing life. Unable to stop them, Alex leans in and uses the newfound accountability to turn his life around.

Title: Minnow
Logline: When her sister goes missing, Minnow, a seemingly troubled woman, goes to extreme lengths to hunt down the mysterious man who took her. The disturbing truth goes beyond just one missing girl, and nothing, including Minnow, is as it seems.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List — #5 overall. IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt39144543/

Title: ALTS
Logline: After years of consequence-free time travel, a man, Guy, discovers his reckless behavior has created dozens of alternate versions of himself who all want him dead.

Title: Kampf
Logline: An actor from a hit medical show disappears for years before reappearing in history-channel reenactments playing one role repeatedly: Joseph Goebbels. His reasons remain unknown until he meets a young girl dying of cancer through Make-A-Wish.

Title: Revenge Body
Logline: Fifteen years after a high school prank ruined his life, a now-unrecognizable personal trainer sets his sights on destroying the lives of the group of former students responsible.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List. Inspired by 'The Count of Monte Cristo' by Alexandre Dumas.

Title: Blackout
Logline: When eco-terrorists attack Los Angeles' power grid, a jaded former Secret Service agent and a brilliant but unassuming engineer must fight their way across the metropolis, in the dark, to restore power before the city collapses.

Title: Sunlight
Logline: A husband and wife's marriage is tested when the husband is bitten by a bat and becomes a vampire.

Title: Mr. Blackburn
Logline: A passionate inner-city high school teacher discovers he's accidentally inspired his former students into meaningless minimum-wage work and insurmountable student debt. Together they pick up a life of crime to pay off their debts and realize the American dream he promised.

Title: The Parole Officer
Logline: A parolee trying to stay on the straight and narrow is seduced by a mysterious woman into a drug-fueled one-night stand that violates his parole. She turns out to be his newly appointed parole officer, who blackmails him into an escalating series of crimes or else back to prison for life.

Title: Infestation
Logline: A suburban family's life spirals into horror after their young son is bitten by a strange beetle and begins transforming into a monstrous insect. As a massive infestation infects their home, the father must confront the darkness in his past to save his son.

Title: Handy Man
Logline: When a frustrated stay-at-home father in Brooklyn feels increasingly threatened by the charismatic handyman hired by his wife, their simmering rivalry escalates into a volatile battle of wills that threatens to unravel their families.

Title: Rush
Logline: When a college student is sexually assaulted by a frat guy who faces no consequences, she and her best friend rush his fraternity undercover to get revenge, only to become the unlikely stars of the pledge class and get in way too deep.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List — #3 overall. IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt39144533/

Title: Frostbite
Logline: There are 300 frozen bodies on the way to the summit of Mt. Everest. For blind climber Lucas Mills, he can still hear them. A survival-horror experience on Everest.

Title: Place to Be
Logline: James, a good dad who flies a bit too close to the sun, hasn't seen his kids in 10 years, but not by choice. When he makes his grand return at his daughter's destination wedding and no one recognizes him, he must fight to win his family's trust so he can walk her down the aisle.

Title: Renegotiate
Logline: A troubled FBI crisis negotiator finds himself stuck in a time loop, re-living the events of a suicide bombing and struggling to stop it before time runs out.

Title: Out of the Hollow
Logline: In need of funds for his father's medical care, Danny Locascio enlists his mercurial best friend Rusty in a series of jewel store robberies. The pair soon find themselves in the crosshairs of both the FBI and local organized crime as old secrets unravel.

Title: Capricorn
Logline: A married couple spice things up by starting a sexual relationship with a mysterious young woman, and it entangles them in a web of deception and danger.

Title: Bad Memories
Logline: After surviving a rival tribe's raid, a pregnant Mesolithic woman forms an unlikely alliance with a wolf in order to survive the unforgiving prehistoric wilderness.

Title: Heartland Express
Logline: In rural Wyoming, a stressed-out single mom on the brink of an empty nest gets dragged into disastrous dates, honky-tonk nights, family blowups, and a new, possibly-fated romance, pushing her to discover that the hardest road to navigate is the one back to herself.

Title: Wild Palms
Logline: An ambitious grifter and a seasoned reptile hunter form an unlikely partnership to exploit Florida's exotic wildlife for profit, but their risky black-market ventures lead them into a world of danger, betrayal, and life-altering consequences.

Title: Lettered Men
Logline: A group of fraternity brothers wake up after a night of partying nobody can remember to find a dead body in the basement: the fraternity president's ex-girlfriend. Each brother has equal cause, evidence against them, and lack of alibi.

Title: ET AL
Logline: When a husband-and-wife research team, along with their dangerously charming old college friend, venture into the Congo jungle in search of a brand-new species, the expedition quickly unravels into one of paranoia and terror.

Title: The Pirate
Logline: On the storm-tossed seas of the 18th century, a fisherman infiltrates a murderous pirate warship.

Title: Riding Hurt
Logline: A former rodeo star, caught between his criminal past and a fragile shot at redemption, is drawn into a dangerous run of heists and betrayals while trying to find a way back to his family.

Title: Lord of the Dance
Logline: When a 35-year-old Chicago ditch digger gets the chance to represent his ancestral homeland at the world's biggest music competition, he unleashes a dance phenomenon that will make Ireland a global superpower and finally set his people free.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List. Based on the true story of Riverdance.

Title: Early Action
Logline: Two parents go to extreme lengths to get their son into his dream college, even if it means manufacturing a traumatic experience for him to write about in his admissions essay.

Title: Oh Yoko!
Logline: Born in war-torn Tokyo, Yoko Ono forges her path as a groundbreaking modern artist in New York, but her life changes forever when she dives into a whirlwind romance with John Lennon and becomes known as the woman who broke up the Beatles.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List. Biopic of Yoko Ono.

Title: The Stag and the Bull
Logline: Desperate to revive their marriage, a suburban couple invites a stranger into their bedroom, only to discover that some fantasies can really kill the mood.

Title: With the 8th Pick
Logline: In the 1996 NBA draft, New Jersey Nets GM John Nash fights against his own organization in his push to take an untested high schooler named Kobe Bryant.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List. Based on the true story of the 1996 NBA draft (Kobe Bryant).

Title: Do It Yourself
Logline: Tom and Helen Hempel, hosts of a wildly popular home renovation show, are America's favorite couple. When Tom dies in his mistress Melody's bed, polar opposites Helen and Melody decide to move his body home to conceal the scandal. What could go wrong?

Title: Geezers
Logline: After surviving a prom night massacre over 50 years ago, Alice's golden years are interrupted by a copycat killer terrorizing her small retirement community.

Title: The Valley of Hinnom
Logline: When a rudderless teenager in an extremist group goes undercover at a synagogue he's plotting to bomb, he finds himself relying on the very people he is supposed to destroy.

Title: Untitled Erotic Teen Fan Fiction Movie
Logline: Delaney Pitts is a nerdy teenage virgin with a secret online life as an erotic fan-fiction author. When a publisher tasks her with writing a book about her (non-existent) high school love life, she's forced to team up with a top expert in the field: the slutty quarterback of the football team.

Title: American Midnight
Logline: When several bodies are found along the highways of the American Midwest, a divorced state trooper and her estranged FBI husband must work together to find a trucker murdering young women for his sick wife... a vampire.

Title: Sister
Logline: Sister Molly is not a Sister anymore, not after falling in love and leaving the convent behind. But two years later, when her relationship ends, she returns to the convent to figure out who she idolizes more: God, or her ex-boyfriend.

Title: Capable People
Logline: While hosting a fundraiser for a razor-thin election, a morally conflicted congresswoman and her staff desperately try to contain a ballooning scandal that threatens her career, her marriage, and the balance of power in Washington D.C.

Title: Don't Do 72
Logline: A night janitor is warned never to clean the 72nd floor of a corporate high-rise, but when a coworker disappears, she breaks the rule, only to discover something ancient and watching, lurking just beyond the glass.

Title: Equity
Logline: After an ambitious pharma founder sells a portion of himself to a charismatic billionaire magnate, his world spirals into a high-stakes battle to buy back control of his company, his future, and his life.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List — #2 overall. IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt39144377/

Title: Standby
Logline: On her final voyage as a flight attendant, a woman finds herself trapped on a trans-Pacific flight with a passenger who is adamant that they're perfect together and will do everything he can to win her over.

Title: Crush
Logline: When a woman goes for a solo hike in the Everglades and falls, she wakes up with a massive snake curled around her ankles, ready to slowly kill her.

Title: Entertaining
Logline: Cooking show superstar Celeste Bell and her upstart rival Josie Baker vie for food media's most coveted broadcast, Thanksgiving, over the course of a decade.

Title: Fixation
Logline: A couples therapist is drawn into a dangerous triangle of lust, lies, and manipulation when she begins an affair with a stranger who turns out to be the husband of her new client.

Title: Serpent Girl
Logline: Waking up naked by the highway, tripping on peyote with a slashed throat and no memory, Bailey Quinn slowly recalls it all: the circus, the heist, the betrayal. His bloody bender for retribution unexpectedly leads him to love and an unexpected state of grace.

Title: House of Time
Logline: When an eccentric billionaire invites five guests to an extravagant weekend at his secluded chateau, what starts as an elaborate time-travel game quickly turns deadly real. Or so we think...
Notes: On the 2025 Black List. IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt37898592/

Title: Best Seller
Logline: In New York's literary scene, a struggling writer, pressured by her famous novelist husband to have a baby, pens a tell-all article that goes viral, sparking a dangerous battle of seduction, manipulation, and betrayal in the public and private spheres.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List — #1 overall. IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt39144364/

Title: Nick of Time
Logline: While defending an unscrupulous billionaire in a landmark antitrust lawsuit, a tenacious young attorney begins to question his sanity as he unravels a time-bending secret from the past that could threaten his future.

Title: Object Permanence
Logline: A Berkeley professor's wife mysteriously disappears immediately after he is wrongfully accused in a Me Too scandal. His life explodes into a spectacular comedy of errors as he races to find her and society rushes to convict him of murder.

Title: Incidents
Logline: A woman searches for answers after surviving a mysterious abduction.

Title: The Milkman
Logline: After his beloved cow is senselessly killed, a peaceful dairy farmer becomes a vengeance-obsessed one-man wrecking crew, taking on a corrupt conglomerate and the violent enforcers who protect it.

Title: The Light from the Arcade
Logline: On a nostalgic hometown visit, a disillusioned man is sucked into his favorite childhood arcade game, where he must team up with his teenage self to fight a horde of mind-bending monsters and find a way home, confronting their past to save their future.

Title: Last Stop in the Desert
Logline: A disgraced journalist visits a hippie commune to rekindle an old flame, but when a body turns up in the middle of his first acid trip, he investigates, even as the trip turns his search for truth into a kaleidoscope of clues, visions, and lies.

Title: Dead Man's Island
Logline: A pirate is marooned on a mysterious island, only to discover that he's being stalked by a bloodthirsty creature.

Title: Peaches
Logline: Molly, a loveable mess, newly divorced and drinking way too much, finds the unlikely lifeline she needs after one reckless night: Peaches, an enormous St. Bernard rescue dog.

Title: Amphora
Logline: A dramatic thriller that takes place over two parts of a man's life.

Title: Alpha
Logline: A mild-mannered American analyst climbs the ranks of a ruthless London investment firm, only to discover a horror more frightening than the industry itself: the insatiable monster awakening within him.

Title: The Texan
Logline: When a self-made Houston mogul takes a young, ambitious West Texas striver under his wing, he ignites psychological warfare between mentor and protege over fortune, family, reputation, and life-and-death.

Title: Guys With No Friends
Logline: When four friendless, middle-aged men are pushed into a 'man date' by their fed-up wives, they stumble into a night of misadventures that leaves them bruised, bonded, and forever changed.

Title: Building Bowie
Logline: In a near-future obsessed with AI-generated nostalgia, a repressed droid wrangler is assigned to build a David Bowie replica. As the droid evolves beyond its programming, she must confront the creative dreams she buried and decide whether to protect her job or reclaim her voice.

Title: Sugar Free
Logline: When their sugar daddy and sugar mommy dump them to be together, a self-absorbed gold digger and a loveable slacker join forces to break up their exes and get back on easy street.

Title: Pinfall
Logline: Lola, a former wrestler turned manager, finally sees her chance for a big break when she discovers a once-in-a-generation talent, only to risk her shot at success and the people she cares about most as the brutal world of professional wrestling takes its toll.

Title: Unicorn
Logline: When a tech mogul and his wife invite a third into their marriage for a weekend away, their sexually charged celebration devolves into a cat-and-mouse game of deception.

Title: The Black Echo
Logline: After a hostage rescue mission goes wrong, an elite SWAT team is forced into a vast underground tunnel system, cut off and stripped of their tactical advantage by an enemy unlike any they've ever encountered, who's turned the labyrinth into their killing field.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List. Listed on 8FLiX as 'Black Echo, The.'

Title: Untouchable
Logline: The true exploits of Eliot Ness during his hunt for the 'Torso Murderer,' a serial killer whose bloody reign terrorized 1930s Cleveland.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List — #4 overall. Based on the true hunt for Cleveland's 'Torso Murderer.' IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt39144550/

Title: The Waffle House Index
Logline: Three things are true: the Waffle House Index is a metric FEMA uses to measure the severity of a natural disaster; Jane works the graveyard shift at her local Waffle House; and there's a storm coming.

Title: Alter
Logline: In a secret military lab, a neuroscientist uses cutting-edge technology to rewire the mind of a psychopathic killer, forcing her own memories onto him to trigger empathy. As their minds intertwine, buried truths surface and sanity blurs.

Title: The Survival List
Logline: Against her wishes, highbrow reality-TV producer Annie is assigned to a new show hosted by macho survival expert Chopper Lane. When a shipwreck strands them on a deserted island, Annie discovers Chopper is a fraud who knows nothing about survival, leaving her in charge of keeping them alive, and discovering an unlikely chemistry.

Title: Leverage
Logline: After a high-profile murder threatens a multi-billion-dollar hostile takeover, an embattled Wall Street titan emerges as the prime suspect and must win a war of perception to protect her empire at all costs.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List — #8 overall. IMDb: https://www.imdb.com/title/tt39148042/

Title: Flamer
Logline: In his final week at wilderness camp, fifteen-year-old Aiden faces bullies, shifting friendships, and an overwhelming crush in a turbulent reckoning with his identity.
Notes: On the 2025 Black List. Based on the acclaimed graphic novel 'Flamer' by Mike Curato.

Title: Let's Be Friends
Logline: An awkward 30-something woman on a quest for new friends thinks she's hit the mother lode when she's invited on vacation by a seemingly perfect group of lifelong besties, but she instead finds herself on the beach weekend from Hell.

Title: Cut Outs
Logline: A detective whose mind is deteriorating after a tragic misstep becomes obsessed with an unexplainable case: unrelated people keep claiming their bodies have been stolen from them, leaving them stranded as someone else.

Title: Trace
Logline: A forensic sketch artist's life is upended when her latest composite of a serial killer looks identical to her husband of 6 years.

Title: West Coast Living
Logline: When a mysterious van settles outside a middle-class couple's home, a simple noise complaint unleashes a relentless campaign of psychological warfare and escalating violence.

Title: Vienna
Logline: An interpreter at the US Embassy in Vienna must act quickly when she's pulled into a late-night meeting between the American and Russian presidents and discovers that they are negotiating the outcome of a pre-planned nuclear war.

Title: Liana
Logline: An overly ambitious young woman's obsession with a notorious hedge fund manager leads to increasingly disturbing behavior in a quest to get close to, and ultimately take down, her idol.

Title: The 13th Hour
Logline: When a group of teenagers repair an old clock with a mysterious 13th numeral, they are granted an extra hour where their actions have no consequence.

Title: The 7 Guys You Date Before Marriage
Logline: After her ten-year relationship collapses, a newly single woman dives back into the dating pool, navigating seven wildly different romantic entanglements that prepare her for her future husband.

Title: AIDa
Logline: A new mother finds the perfect nanny in AI, only to learn that a "perfect" caregiver might be the most dangerous kind of all. This psychological thriller examines the disparity between human nature versus AI nurture and asks the question, could AI ever replace us as parents?

Title: Alignment
Logline: A board member at a booming AI company wrestles with corporate politics and warped incentives as he tries to prevent his colleagues' willful ignorance from causing a global catastrophe.

Title: American Monsters
Logline: A senator and his estranged son must fight to survive when they're stranded at a hunting ranch where mythical beasts from global folklore are genetically engineered as dangerous game.

Title: Assembly Required
Logline: During a clandestine overnight stay in the Burbank IKEA, four friends get caught in a firefight between rival criminal organizations, forcing them to figure out how to save each other–and the store they love so much.

Title: A Band of Wolves
Logline: After surviving a rival tribe's raid, a pregnant Mesolithic woman forms an unlikely alliance with a wolf in order to survive the unforgiving prehistoric wilderness.

Title: Bato Bato
Logline: When his family is murdered and child kidnapped, a Neanderthal goes on an epic rampage journey to save her from the new dominant species.

Title: Bitter Sweet Symphony
Logline: In the last days of the Britpop era, enigmatic musician Richard Ashcroft's quest to transform an obscure Rolling Stones cover into the iconic single "Bitter Sweet Symphony" sparks a legal battle that threatens to both destroy his band The Verve and silence the song forever.

Title: Bloody Mingo
Logline: Investigating the crime scene of a potential homicide in the Appalachian mountainside, a deputy encounters a strange mountain community that claims to be haunted by an evil witch that lives in the woods.

Title: Bo Knows Infinity
Logline: Bo Jackson gets stuck in a time loop. Lessons are learned.

Title: Boy, Girl, Fig
Logline: Aden was born with a rare condition where he becomes invisible to those who love him. He struggles when he falls in love with his childhood best friend.

Title: Bridgehampton
Logline: Recently dumped Ezra Green accidentally brings a terminally ill woman home to Bridgehampton for a long weekend with his eccentric family. Don't judge -- he needs to cope with his estranged father who just got out of white-collar prison.

Title: The Busboy
Logline: After a mysterious busboy manipulates his way into a job at the most exclusive nightclub in South Beach, he quickly rises through the ranks using ruthless tactics.

Title: Bust
Logline: In 1991, Houston, Texas, millions of women sport the latest fashion trend: breast implants. But when they develop life-threatening side effects, they'll need a special kind of lawyer to seek justice. This is the incredible true story of a women's public health crisis, a gun-slinging plaintiff lawyer, and the biggest lawsuit in American history.
Notes: On the 2024 Black List — #10 overall. Based on a true story.

Title: Camp David
Logline: In 1981, a young George W. Bush and his siblings are hunted by a masked killer while partying at Camp David.

Title: Clean Break
Logline: Mary, a hair trigger pool hustler, has her hedonistic lifestyle all figured out until she meets Ray, a fellow pool shark. Will she change her ways and let herself fall in love? Or just kill him and make a break for it...

Title: Crush
Logline: A woman wakes up in a room with no recollection of how she got there. The ceiling descends.

Title: Decoys
Logline: When two cocky self-proclaimed CIA bad-asses learn they're being used as diversions, they must prove to themselves and the world that they're capable of being real heroes.

Title: Detour
Logline: When a feuding rap group's tour bus breaks down in the middle of nowhere, three hip hop superstars find themselves locked in a life-or-death struggle to survive the night as they are hunted by a group of locals with a hidden agenda involved.

Title: Diana Destroys a Wedding
Logline: When her youngest son proposes to a middle-class farmer, New York business icon Diana Ivy incentivizes her dysfunctional children to protect the family name the only way she knows how... by creating a competition: whoever can break up the couple during their luxurious seven-day destination wedding will inherit the Ivys' Lake Como estate.

Title: Do Not Disturb
Logline: When a bumbling pretty-boy criminal screws up a job, he and his scruffy partner decide to lay low at a luxury LA hotel where they cross paths with a British playwright and a Hollywood starlet.

Title: Dog
Logline: Lukas and Sofia's new life in a small town is shattered when they return home one night to find their dog gruesomely killed–was it a wild animal, a neighbour or a sinister, supernatural force from the valley? Desperate for the truth, Lukas embarks on a witch hunt after evidence points to a young local boy–but as they are plagued by further unexplained, malevolent events, the family unit unravels with devastating consequences.

Title: Don't Borrow Trouble
Logline: A broke TaskRabbit in debt to her estranged sugar daddy holds a stolen painting she's been tasked to deliver for ransom, leading to a deadly cat-and-mouse chase across the weirdest corners of New York City.

Title: Dyersville
Logline: Two Federal Marshals guarding the most important cooperating witness in Chicago's history -- a legendary gangster–are forced to reckon with their own complicated pasts and with the first mole in the history of the Witness Protection Program. Inspired by true events.
Notes: On the 2024 Black List. Inspired by true events.

Title: Ex-Cons
Logline: A former husband-and-wife jewel thief duo, now divorced and in jail, must team up to help the authorities catch the most elusive thief of all: their son.

Title: Fellowship
Logline: A burgeoning artist is offered a once in a lifetime fellowship, luring her into the dark side of New York's elite art world.

Title: Fragments
Logline: After a tragic accident, a young nurse finds herself blacklisted and out of work until she's approached by a detective with a unique job offer–become the caretaker for a murder suspect who may be faking his Alzheimer's diagnosis.

Title: Golden Boy
Logline: To save his cash-strapped soccer club, the owner of SSC Napoli stakes his reputation and taxpayers' dollars on a mercurial, drug-addicted star named Diego Maradona.

Title: Good For Us
Logline: After surviving a terrifying home invasion with her two young children, a woman leans on her husband for support, but soon suspects that it was not a random incident and that her husband might have somehow been involved.

Title: Grave Expectations
Logline: After the recession hits their funeral business hard, a desperate mother is convinced by her manipulative daughter to start a risky side hustle–selling body parts. Based on a true story.
Notes: On the 2024 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: How to Save a Marriage
Logline: In hopes of spicing up their marriage, a husband and wife join a mysterious couples retreat, only to discover it's a front for a dangerous cult.

Title: Howl
Logline: All hell breaks loose when a famous–but notoriously troubled–actor announces on a talk show that in less than an hour, he'll turn into a werewolf... all on live TV.

Title: Intimacy
Logline: A rising movie star and her struggling playwright husband meet with a pretentious director and a manipulative intimacy coordinator to rehearse a sex scene. Over one chaotic day, power struggles, petty jealousies, and explosive accusations threaten their marriage–and the careers of everyone involved.

Title: Is This You
Logline: Maura and her mother Jeanne have always had a tricky relationship. But when Jeanne starts publishing essays about her troubled daughter's teenage years, those versions of her appear like hormone-fueled poltergeists hell-bent on ruining the real Maura's tenuous adult stability.

Title: It's Not Me, It's You
Logline: When Nick and Sophie break up just days before the lavish destination wedding of his best friend and her sister, they vow to keep it a secret until after the nuptials. But what should be a simple white lie becomes a living nightmare when a mystical encounter turns them into the one thing they hate most in the world: each other.

Title: King of the Freshman
Logline: During the glittering ascent of Abercrombie & Fitch, a bold CEO and his enigmatic British partner craft an empire of desire, exclusivity, and allure–only to see their legacy unravel as a scandal of exploitation and power threatens to expose the dangerous lengths they went to in their pursuit of perfection.

Title: Little Black Dress
Logline: While on hiatus from filming "Breakfast at Tiffany's," international movie star Audrey Hepburn is recruited by the CIA to hunt down a Nazi criminal hiding in Buenos Aires with ties to her past.

Title: Love of Your Life
Logline: Maya -- a smart, wry nurse–is swept up in an immersive love, one that sets her life on a trajectory she never would have imagined.

Title: Mildred
Logline: Years after being banished from her family, an aging matriarch returns to the family business to save her estranged children from a deadly criminal syndicate.

Title: Mom?
Logline: When a deadly virus infects mothers and turns them against their offspring, a father must do whatever it takes to protect his daughter from her mom.

Title: Mommy's Home
Logline: A young dad revives his 24-year-old cryogenically frozen mom, unleashing terror and forbidden tension that haunt his family and threaten to unravel his marriage.

Title: Motherboy
Logline: When a woman becomes trapped in a snowstorm with her overbearing mother-in-law and husband over the Thanksgiving holiday, she begins to suspect that their relationship is not as maternal as it seems. BARBARIAN by way of an erotic, Hallmark holiday movie.

Title: Nasty
Logline: After an injury sidelines Dylan's ability to train for the Olympics, the determined gymnast must regain the trust of her demanding coach while facing off against a prodigious new competitor threatening to eclipse her place in the spotlight.

Title: NDA
Logline: Through a maddening day of mediation for an office harassment claim she filed, the clock is ticking on Dana as she questions the price of her silence, sanity, and the truth.

Title: Old Souls
Logline: A trio of sassy, elderly women receive a unique offer from Death for a week of youth in exchange for their lives.

Title: Onboarding!
Logline: When a group of new hires get invited to their company's corporate retreat, things quickly take a turn as they discover the only way to land the job is to survive the weekend. Literally.

Title: Out There
Logline: Fresh off a breakup, Meg is thrilled to meet Roger, who seems like the perfect man: disarmingly handsome, sweet, and obsessed with her. But she'll soon discover that a disturbing secret lies behind Roger's perfection, and will have to decide how far she's willing to go on behalf of true love–even if the person she loves happens to be fake.
Notes: On the 2024 Black List. Based on the short stories 'Out There' and 'Big Sur' by Kate Folk.

Title: Over Asking
Logline: After a chance encounter with a powerful female litigator, a seemingly perfect couple begin to ask questions about marriage, power, gender, and sex.

Title: Paparazzo
Logline: Desperate for fast cash to help his ailing mother, an opportunistic outcast partners with a wily paparazzo who entangles them in a homicide involving young celebutantes.

Title: A Payroll to Meet
Logline: Based on David Whitford's best-selling book chronicling the true story of college sports' greatest scandal, a Dallas real estate tycoon spearheads the rise-and-fall of SMU football's Pony Express.
Notes: On the 2024 Black List. Based on David Whitford's book on the SMU football 'Pony Express' scandal.

Title: Playdate
Logline: When taking her daughter to a playdate at a new friend's house, Alice suddenly comes face to face with her childhood bully, Katrine. An evening of seemingly polite dinner conversation and catching up turns into a night of psychological warfare as the two women reveal the scars of the past and the wounds of the present while their two young daughters play mind games of their own.

Title: Ride or Die
Logline: A debt-ridden Peloton instructor finds his entire class held hostage by a bomber during one of his live-in-studio classes.

Title: Ripe!
Logline: Nothing says "it's complicated" like breaking your crush's arm. Set in rural Spain against the backdrop of a passionate soccer rivalry, the story follows young protagonists Sophie and Gloria as they navigate their relationship.

Title: Road Test
Logline: A young woman long overdue in getting her driver's license finally takes her road test, only to find herself receiving direction from a psychotic instructor who wants her dead.

Title: The Road to Cold Valley
Logline: In search of a lost family heirloom, a graduate student and his friends take a road trip to the ruins of the remote Japanese American internment camp where his ancestors were incarcerated during World War II. But after a car breakdown and a series of misunderstandings, the group must fight for their lives as they are stalked across the desert at night by an unhinged, racist mechanic.

Title: Rot
Logline: Haunted by the death of his hoarder mother, an antisocial man suffering from obsessive compulsions takes work as a trauma cleaner in hopes of facing his past, but the job soon begins to infest and unravel his mental state.

Title: Saturn Return
Logline: Eve and Anders dated for a decade. Now, Eve is going to see Anders for the first time in years... at his dad's funeral. Together, they confront their shared past and the infinite nature of love, even after it dies.

Title: Shutout
Logline: A talented young pool player from the Bronx navigates the male-dominated world of pool hustling, guided by a seasoned gambler, as she rises to international fame and grapples with the moral complexities of the game.

Title: Skin
Logline: When famed Beverly Hills dermatologist Amelia Williams' status begins to slip as she ages, she makes a revolutionary discovery that allows her to completely revitalize her appearance and maintain her lifestyle. It just requires a bit of murder every now and then.

Title: Spin the Bottle
