Logline: When a talented hacker is recruited by the mysterious Cicada 3301, she gets wrapped up in a plot that threatens to destroy the entire world. Based on the real organization.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List — #4 overall. Based on the real Cicada 3301.

Title: The Diamond as Big as the Ritz
Logline: When the underprivileged John Unger is invited to spend the summer at the mansion of his peculiar classmate, his thirst for grandeur leads him down a dangerous exploration of greed, morality, and the secret horrors of the ruling class.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. Based on the F. Scott Fitzgerald short story.

Title: Doll Wars
Logline: When the upstart Bratz dolls challenged Barbie's antiquated gender norms and threatened their monopoly, Barbie struck back with a billion-dollar copyright lawsuit. In jeopardy of shutting down, Bratz turned to a small, scrappy, all-female law firm to take on Mattel in a historic, David vs. Goliath intellectual property battle that changed the toy industry and American popular culture forever.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. Based on a true story (Bratz vs. Mattel).

Title: Dollhouse
Logline: When a struggling fashion model in New York City gets chosen by a mysterious Parisian designer to be the face of his first campaign since his disappearance five years prior, she begins to realize she was chosen for a reason and must decide how much she's willing to sacrifice for beauty and recognition.

Title: Don't Go in the Water
Logline: A recovering alcoholic sets off for seclusion but gets more than he bargained for when he encounters a mysterious creature nearby.

Title: Field of View
Logline: A soldier, forced to relive her worst day in combat, begins to question her sanity when the VR simulation she's experiencing doesn't match her memory of the mission gone wrong.

Title: First Ascent
Logline: Two years after a free solo accident nearly kills Hillary Hall, she enlists the help of her old climbing partners to document her comeback -- the first ascent of a 4,000 foot rock wall in rural China. During the harrowing climb, Hillary struggles with her inner demons and supernatural forces, as it slowly becomes clear that this mountain does not want to be conquered.

Title: First Harvest
Logline: George runs a struggling farm where he cares for his terminally ill wife, Alice. Everything changes when he decides to bring home an orphaned baby he found out in the field. The child's presence begins to unearth buried secrets while engulfing the family in a series of supernatural events.

Title: Girlfriend on Mars
Logline: Amber and Kevin, weed dealer burnouts committed to going nowhere together, have been dating for twelve years. When Amber enters a reality show that will take the winner to Mars with no chance of return, Kevin has to face what it really means when the ones we love leave us and leave the planet.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. Based on the story by Deborah Willis.

Title: Grandma Wants to Die
Logline: When Ben is left footing the bill for his own wedding just weeks before the big day, he's forced to make a deal with his estranged grandmother Minnie. She'll give him the money if he signs the papers for her assisted suicide. Ben gladly agrees, only to unearth Minnie's final agenda before she departs... destroy the wedding from the inside and seemingly ruin Ben's life.

Title: Helldiver
Logline: After finding themselves stranded on the wreckage of a Helldiver bomber in the middle of the ocean, an American aviator and a Japanese Kamikaze pilot must work together to survive their greatest threat yet -- a 22-foot great white shark.

Title: High on Christmas
Logline: A stoner comedy about one family trying to save Christmas from itself after Santa eats the wrong batch of cookies.

Title: The House Is Not For Sale
Logline: With one last chance at a promotion, a down-on-her-luck real estate agent returns to her rural hometown to sell the impossible - a haunted house where countless couples have been murdered. As the bodies of new residents continue to pile up, our real estate agent will stop at nothing to rid the house of evil - no matter what the cost.

Title: I Heart Murder
Logline: A true-crime podcaster tries to solve a gruesome cold case, putting her in the killer's crosshairs.

Title: Klein
Logline: Life as a single dad hasn't been a challenge for Las Vegas blackjack dealer Mike Klein, until his ex resurfaces after walking out on the family six years ago.

Title: The Laborer
Logline: A pair of out-of-work immigrant brothers catch a break when they are hired as day laborers to work at a house in the Hollywood Hills, until they witness something they will wish they had never seen.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. | Also on ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25' (all-time; ~#20; rated 'impressive' — Reeves said it should've been #1 on the 2019 list, as of Jul 2026).

Title: A Magical Place Called Glendale
Logline: To revamp her self image, an arrogant but well-meaning high school socialite decides to help a former friend land the guy of her dreams... but in the process, realizes she wants her for herself.

Title: The Man in the Woods
Logline: After moving to Maine and befriending an enigmatic hermit, twelve-year-old Henrietta Thorne begins to wonder if he holds the key to solving a mystery that has eluded our planet for more than a decade.

Title: Meet Cute
Logline: MEET CUTE, the hottest dating app on the market, brings couples together by giving them their Rom Com moment. When the app's biggest skeptic, Haley, matches with one of its developers, Russ, their instant connection starts to change her mind.

Title: The Mother
Logline: The incredible true story of Fredericka Marm Mandelbaum, the country's first female mob boss. During the Gilded Age, Marm opened a school for criminals and built her empire by treating her gang of runaways and orphans as family -- eventually partnering with George Leslie to pull off what is still to date the largest bank heist in American history (adjusting for inflation).
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. Based on the true story of Fredericka 'Marm' Mandelbaum.

Title: Move On
Logline: Teddy thinks he's the only living person left in a world where humanity is frozen in time... until his ex-girlfriend, Leyna, shows up at his doorstep. Together, they must go on a journey to find the cause behind the freeze and in the process, confront the issues that plagued their relationship before it's too late.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List — #1 overall (top-voted script of 2019).

Title: My Boyfriend's Wedding
Logline: After Georgia accidentally receives an out of the blue invitation to her on-again, off-again boyfriend's wedding, she and her best friend Keely make the ill-informed decision to attend.

Title: No Good Deed
Logline: A woman with a troubled past invites her teen niece to live with her in the family's farm house, but the two become tormented by a creature that can take away their pain for a price.

Title: Nomads
Logline: A reverse SEARCHERS from the perspective of the Natives going East into the unknown, the metropolis, the belly of the beast, late 1800s New York City.

Title: The Perdition in Liége
Logline: A prisoner of war in a Belgian POW camp survives an execution firing squad by chance and escapes by hiding among the dead. After removing his fallen compatriots' dog tags to give their families solace, he attempts the fifty-mile journey south toward the allied stronghold, through the deadly war-torn landscape, and before a hell-bent SS officer on his tail catches up to him.

Title: Pod
Logline: After a mission to destroy a black hole that endangers mankind goes wrong, an astronaut awakens in her escape pod to find that decades have passed seemingly in a moment. Now, with an old body and fragile mind, she battles against the elements of space & time to complete her mission.

Title: The Process
Logline: Trapped at a three day personal development retreat, a woman fights to save her husband and herself from being brainwashed by a charismatic self-help guru.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. | Also on ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25' (all-time; ~#18, as of Jul 2026).

Title: Refuge
Logline: A brother and sister navigate the perils of both man and nature through Central America in their quest to find safety in the United States.

Title: The Repossession
Logline: Twenty years after a failed exorcism, a meek young woman becomes unlikely friends with the foul-mouthed demon that possessed her as a child.

Title: Ripple
Logline: After strange deaths and suicides skyrocket in a dying Appalachian coal town, Maggie - a first responder - wages a personal war against the local coal mine, unearthing a disturbing past that the company has kept secret within the waters of the local lake.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. (Distinct from the 2022 script 'Ripple' by Max Taxe.)

Title: Rumours
Logline: In 1970s LA, Stevie Nicks and Lindsey Buckingham, two struggling musicians and lovers, join a band called Fleetwood Mac and are thrown into a whirlwind of worldwide fame, infamous drug addiction, and one of the best-selling albums of all time -- which also happens to be about the disintegration of their relationship.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List — #7 overall. Based on Fleetwood Mac (Stevie Nicks & Lindsey Buckingham).

Title: Say Something Nice
Logline: After she catches her boyfriend cheating, Liv goes on a social media tirade that lands her in court for slander, and the judge forces her to make amends by composing one positive comment for every negative comment she posted, while under the supervision of a reclusive mediator also on the rebound from a failed relationship.

Title: The Searchers
Logline: An inside look at the unique relationship between John Wayne and John Ford on the set of THE SEARCHERS.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. Based on the making of THE SEARCHERS (John Wayne & John Ford).

Title: The Showrunner
Logline: The unbelievable true story of the creation of the world's first, longest running, and most notorious reality show 'COPS.' Based on 'How Cops Got Made — And What It Says About America' by Tim Stelloh.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. Based on the true story of the reality show COPS.

Title: Sister
Logline: Following her mother's sudden passing, a queer black woman returns to her southern town to assist her estranged sister plan the funeral. Her trip takes a turn when sleep deprivation manifests visions of her deceased mother.

Title: Stampede
Logline: Set on a southern plantation in the 1800s, a young slave girl named Lena has telekinetic powers she cannot yet control. Circumstances escalate when she's separated from her mother to be a house girl, in close quarters with the mercurial Master's wife: Elizabeth.

Title: Super Dad
Logline: A subversive superhero story about the world's only superhero living a bachelor lifestyle, learning he has two very different teenage twins he never knew existed, and now has to figure out how to be a father.

Title: The Swells
Logline: A young woman, propelled by an unstoppable rage, begins inviting her victims to a summer lake house as revenge for past wrongs. But when one of her guests has been wronged as well, she passes on The Swells - bringing its wrath to the streets of New York.

Title: T
Logline: The true story of how Lawrence Tureaud, a poor kid in Chicago with 12 brothers and sisters, fought and charmed his way into becoming the single lettered icon of saintly compassion and macho energy.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. Based on the life of Mr. T (Lawrence Tureaud).

Title: This Is Going to Hurt
Logline: An absent mother attempts to reconnect with her daughter by relaying to her how she helped her own parent through battles with cancer and addiction.

Title: Til Death
Logline: Swept up in the excitement of her wedding day, Dr. Julie Wheeler is oblivious to the killer on her guest list, who is methodically stalking her nearest and dearest, until it's too late.

Title: The Traveler
Logline: A man jumps forward in time at the same time every morning. As the length of time increases for every jump, he struggles to keep his family together and find a cure, all as his secret spreads throughout the world.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List — #5 overall. Based on the novel by Joseph Eckert.

Title: Voicemails for Isabelle
Logline: A low-level TV writer struggles to cope with the death of her little sister by continuing to leave her voicemails chronicling the shitshow that is dating in LA. When the phone number is unknowingly transferred, a cocky New York real estate agent begins receiving the hilarious and confessional voicemails, and feels pulled to California to find this stranger he feels intimately close to.
Notes: On the 2019 Black List. (PDF listed as unavailable on 8FLiX.)

Title: Wayward
Logline: The wife of a megachurch pastor seeks atonement after she and her lover kill an attacker in self-defense, but don't report it out of fear of exposing their affair.

Title: (Please) Maternity Leave
Logline: Two friends try to get their third friend pregnant so they can stop hanging out with her.

Title: The 29th Accident
Logline: A young man tragically loses his wife on the day of their wedding. He is devastated, until four years later on their wedding anniversary, he awakens to find his beloved wife alive and well beside him.

Title: 73 Seconds
Logline: The extraordinary true story of Bob Ebeling and Roger Boisjoly, the aerospace engineers who discovered the 'o-ring anomaly' that led to the Space Shuttle Challenger disaster. After struggling to convince their superiors at Morton-Thiokol and NASA to investigate the anomaly, they were tasked with proving it: a complicated, expensive endeavor that took an emotional toll but helped bring about changes to the Shuttle Program that saved future astronauts.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of the Challenger disaster (Bob Ebeling & Roger Boisjoly).

Title: AMA (Ask Me Anything)
Logline: A highly publicized AMA session between a fast-rising publicist and an aging music icon quickly turns into a deadly game of cat and mouse when the event is seized by a hacker who systematically begins revealing dark secrets from both of their pasts, forcing them to publicly confront the horrific events they've committed on the largest social media platform in the world.

Title: Analytica
Logline: The true story of Chris Wylie and Cambridge Analytica.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List — #9 overall. Based on the true story of Chris Wylie and Cambridge Analytica.

Title: At Risk
Logline: Told through the eyes of ten year old Charlie in 1986, a family struggles to stay intact when an AIDS diagnosis makes them pariahs in their small New England town.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on Alice Hoffman's novel.

Title: Bag Man
Logline: Atticus Archer is college football's version of a fixer. As he juggles chasing the nation's top prospect, a mental breakdown from a star quarterback, and his own personal demons, an NCAA agent arrives on campus to threaten everything.

Title: Beast
Logline: After a plane crash in the Pacific Ocean, a woman finds herself marooned on an island with a bloodthirsty beast.

Title: The Biscuit
Logline: In 1998, a teacher and her veteran husband find a way to afford the family they've always dreamed of: by selling America's nuclear launch codes after President Bill Clinton loses them.

Title: Blur
Logline: When a guy in a committed relationship starts to suspect that the avatar he's been digitally hooking up with might belong to his best friend's girlfriend, he becomes entangled in something he can't quite understand.

Title: Bolsa Negra
Logline: A down and out sports agent risks everything to bring a star Cuban baseball player back to the United States to play in the Major Leagues. Based on an amalgamation of true stories detailing the rise of the movement to smuggle Cuban baseball players into the US during the 1990s.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on true stories of smuggling Cuban baseball players in the 1990s.

Title: The Broodmare
Notes: On the 2018 Black List — #10 overall. (8FLiX logline is a placeholder — full logline unavailable.)

Title: Conviction
Logline: The incredible true story of the final trial of Clarence Darrow, the greatest attorney of the 20th century who for the first and only time in his career was fighting for the wrong side. Darrow defends a wealthy white family caught murdering a native Hawaiian boy whom they wrongfully accused of raping their daughter.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of Clarence Darrow's final trial (the Massie case).

Title: Dark
Logline: When the crew of an oil rig begins to drill on one of the deepest stretches of the ocean floor, they awake a dark and dangerous creature that has been hidden away for hundreds of years.

Title: Dead Dads Club
Logline: A high schooler, in an effort to find a more interesting story for her college scholarship application, lies about her father's recent death. But when the father tries to take advantage of the lie by faking his own death, the high schooler's nemesis investigates, and bodies start piling up.

Title: The Defender
Logline: At the height of Jim Crow, a courageous black attorney with the audacious name of Scipio Africanus Jones risks his life and career to defend eighty-seven men wrongfully accused of murder, taking the case all the way to the United States Supreme Court and achieving a stunning victory that will change American history forever. Based on a true story.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of Scipio Africanus Jones.

Title: Drudge
Logline: The story of how oddball internet reporter Matt Drudge broke the Lewinsky Scandal and nearly took down a presidency, all from a desktop computer in his one-bedroom apartment in Hollywood.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List — #4 overall. Based on the true story of Matt Drudge.

Title: Escher
Logline: Famed artist M.C. Escher reluctantly uses his unique view of the world to help the Dutch Resistance fight Nazi occupation during WWII. Inspired by the life and art of M.C. Escher.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Inspired by the life of M.C. Escher.

Title: The Fastest Game
Logline: Miami, 1975. Ronnie Weiss, a law school graduate primed to start his life, is suddenly saddled with his late father's enormous gambling debt. To climb out of the hole and escape a notorious Miami Beach bookie, Ronnie and a childhood friend devise a betting system that exploits the glamorous, high-stakes sport of Jai Alai, making millions of dollars and enemies of the Boston Mob and Federal Government in the process. Based on a true story.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on a true story (a Jai Alai betting scheme).

Title: Frat Boy Genius
Notes: On the 2018 Black List — #1 overall (top-voted script of 2018). (8FLiX logline is a placeholder — full logline unavailable.)

Title: Get Home Safe
Logline: A young woman must get home by herself on Halloween with no cell phone battery and a group of gamergate trolls out to get her.

Title: Grace
Logline: When Grace and her husband Jay retreat to an empty vacation island to escape his grueling political campaign, Grace begins reliving traumatic experiences from her past, forcing her to question what is real. After she discovers that powerful people behind Jay's campaign have been manipulating her experiences, Grace's struggle to retain her sanity becomes an all too real fight for survival.

Title: Gunfight
Logline: A satirical take on the unbelievable but true story of how the NRA changed overnight from an apolitical gun safety and marksmanship club into the most powerful and unhinged lobbying group in Washington, DC.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. A satire based on the true story of the NRA's transformation.

Title: Happy Anniversary
Logline: A couple's wedding anniversary is interrupted by a home invasion.

Title: Happy Little Trees
Logline: After Bill Alexander's long-running show 'The Magic of Oil Painting' was cancelled by PBS and replaced with Bob Ross' show 'The Joy of Oil Painting,' Alexander accuses the soft-spoken afro'd Ross of stealing his act, inciting a bitter dispute that changed the lives of both men forever. Based on a true story.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of Bob Ross and Bill Alexander.

Title: Hare
Logline: What starts as a fun day for a group of friends in the woods turns into a living nightmare for one rabbit.

Title: Harry's All Night Hamburgers
Logline: A down-on-his-luck high school senior discovers that the old roadside diner outside of town is secretly a hangout for parallel universe travelers. He sets off on a mind-bending adventure across the multiverse that takes him beyond his wildest dreams.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List — #5 overall. Based on the short story by Allen Steele.

Title: In Retrospect
Logline: When a man's estranged wife gets lost inside of her own mind during an experimental procedure, he must navigate her subconscious to find her in the memories of their past.

Title: Infidels
Logline: When a US soldier learns that her Army medic brother is being held hostage by a sadistic ISIS executioner, she deserts her military post and enlists the help of a group of female renegade Kurdish fighters to bring justice when their armies won't help them.

Title: Inhuman Nature
Logline: When bio-tech titan Van Danzen is falsely convicted of murdering his business partner, he sends his greatest creation – a spitting-image humanoid robot – to serve a life sentence in his place. However, the humanoid's militarized programming sends him on a rampage to escape prison and hunt those responsible for his creator's set-up and imprisonment.

Title: The Interventionist
Logline: When renowned interventionist Warren Man's daughter, Christy, suffers a relapse and her family is forced to deal with their inner demons at the site of a tragedy that tore them apart, it slowly becomes clear that this is no ordinary relapse, but something much more sinister.

Title: Isleworth
Logline: After their marriage is shattered by the most public sex scandal in sports history, Tiger Woods and Elin Nordegren scramble to pick up the pieces while hiding out from the press and the police in their Florida mansion.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of Tiger Woods and Elin Nordegren.

Title: Just a Girl
Logline: After the United States survives a vampire war, a young human girl going through puberty learns that she may be turning into a vampire.

Title: Just the Facts
Logline: The riveting true story of AJ Daulerio's meteoric rise from obscure sports blogger to Editor-in-Chief of Gawker Media during the wild, heady early days of the digital journalism boom, culminating in the Hulk Hogan sex tape trial, which brought about Gawker's downfall and set a precedent for billionaires to attack the media and free speech.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of AJ Daulerio and Gawker.

Title: Kill the Leopard
Logline: A henchman-turned-terrorist has a bone to pick with an idolized vigilante. When he takes control of a city skyscraper, the hero known as the Leopard comes to the rescue – but when both the terrorist and the Leopard are overtaken by the hostages, a kangaroo court evolves as the hostages navigate their complex histories with the vigilante.

Title: The Kings of Cool
Logline: During segregation in the 1960s American South, a nerdy teen tries to win a student election at an all-black high school, but he'll have to defeat a blossoming badass named Samuel L. Jackson to do so. Based on a true story.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on a true story (young Samuel L. Jackson).

Title: The Liberators
Logline: At the height of World War II, a headstrong African-American tank commander and his crew plow through war-torn Europe, determined to prove themselves as equals and bring Hitler to his knees.

Title: Mamba
Logline: A chronicle of the Kobe Bryant sexual assault case in the early 2000s, based on reports, court transcripts, and police interviews.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the Kobe Bryant sexual assault case.

Title: Me & Tammy Faye at the Betty Ford Clinic
Logline: The unlikely bond between a Latinx transgender woman and the queen of Christian televangelism. Inspired by the very real life of Tammy Faye Bakker.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Inspired by the life of Tammy Faye Bakker.

Title: Naked Is the Best Disguise
Logline: In a near future in which illegal new technology allows specific memories to be removed from one person's brain and inserted into another's, a woman who deals in black market memories is accused of murdering a man she does not remember knowing.

Title: Nobody Nothing Nowhere
Logline: Ruth is one of the non-people: human-looking beings designed and trained for the sole purpose of filling in a realistic world for a bland guy named Dave, the only person to actually exist on Earth. Tired of serving as an extra in someone else's life, she has the audacity to demand a life of her own.

Title: One Night in Mississippi
Logline: An elderly black man in present day with onset dementia must confront the secrets of his past in order to pursue a man, thought deceased, involved in the white supremacist killing of his brother in 1964 Mississippi.

Title: Our Condolences
Logline: A couple navigates their relationship after another couple, with whom they're close, tragically loses their child.

Title: Popular
Logline: A story of power, betrayal, scandal, and deceit – you know, high school.

Title: Queen
Logline: Based on the inspiring true story of the world's first openly transgender high school Prom Queen, Corey Rae.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of Corey Rae.

Title: Queens of the Stoned Age
Logline: Forced out of a fashion industry that exploited her, former model Honey stumbles into dealing weed to make ends meet – and soon discovers a gift for it. Hiring all her model friends to work with her, they quickly become one of the biggest weed dealers in New York City, using their looks and charm to avoid detection. But their success soon brings all the wrong kinds of attention... and they find themselves in a fight for their lives. Based on a true story.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: Ride
Logline: The story of NASA's least likely female candidate, Sally Ride, as she becomes America's most likely hero as the first American woman in space.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the life of Sally Ride.

Title: Rub & Tug
Logline: In 1970s Pittsburgh, Dante 'Tex' Gill runs a massage parlor and steroids operation that challenges the mob, rising to power with the support of the gay community and girlfriend Cynthia.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of Dante 'Tex' Gill.

Title: Saddam & Me
Logline: The true story of the FBI's interrogation of Saddam, carried out by a special agent whose family came to the US as refugees from Lebanon, ultimately leading to the discovery that there were no WMDs in Iraq.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of the FBI's interrogation of Saddam Hussein.

Title: The Second Life of Ben Haskins
Logline: Ben Haskins loses his battle with cancer, leaving his beloved wife Kat a widow. About twenty years later, a mysterious new technology has brought Ben's consciousness back - but into a different body, one belonging to a more recently deceased man. After acclimating to his new self, Ben asks to be reunited with his long lost wife, only to discover she has opted never to see him again. Convinced this must be a mistake, Ben enlists a new friend to help him win back the love of his (former) life.

Title: The Seventh
Logline: A look at the regiment led by Colonel Custer in the days leading up to the disastrous fight at Little Bighorn against Sitting Bull.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Depicts Custer's 7th Cavalry before Little Bighorn.

Title: Sharon
Logline: Sharon Osbourne tells the story of her relationship with Ozzy before they were married.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the life of Sharon Osbourne.

Title: Spark
Logline: When a young African-American woman takes a job at a well-known venture capitalist firm in Silicon Valley, she must figure out a way to succeed in an elitist society where she feels inherently excluded.

Title: Tillman
Logline: When former NFL player Pat Tillman is killed in battle, his family uncovers the truth from the United States government who lied about the circumstances of his death.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the true story of Pat Tillman.

Title: To the Extreme
Logline: From a high school dropout selling used cars in Dallas to having the first hip hop single to top the Billboard charts with Ice Ice Baby, a young Vanilla Ice struggles with stardom, extortion attempts, and selling out as he makes music history.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the life of Vanilla Ice.

Title: The United States of America v. Bill Gates
Logline: An inexperienced and idealistic twentysomething finds himself at the center of the largest anti-trust suit in modern American history when his idol, billionaire behemoth Bill Gates, wages war against his young internet company.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the Microsoft antitrust case.

Title: Untitled Coast Guard
Logline: An inexperienced Coast Guard Captain is put to the test after she unknowingly intercepts one of the world's most dangerous drug lords. With a hurricane bearing down on them, the captain and her crew must defend the ship against a series of unrelenting attacks.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on Hunter Atkins's article on Coast Guard drug busts.

Title: Untitled Syria Project
Logline: A disillusioned and indoctrinated American teenage girl travels to Syria to help ISIS build their Caliphate and, what she has been led to believe, is a better world. Through her relationship with an everyday Syrian family, she will discover the true face of Islam and the courage to fight for what is right against insurmountable odds.

Title: Welcome to the Neighborhood
Logline: When a new job transplants an upper-middle class family from San Francisco into an up-and-coming neighborhood outside of Portland, they don't get the neighborly welcome they were expecting.

Title: Wendi
Logline: When an ambitious girl from the slums of China manipulates her way into marriage with a media tycoon, she becomes one of the most powerful women in the Western world. This is the story of Wendi Deng, second wife of Rupert Murdoch.
Notes: On the 2018 Black List. Based on the life of Wendi Deng.

Title: The Worst Guy of All Time (And the Girl Who Came to Kill Him)
Logline: Barret is a social media influencer, the worst guy ever, and the eventual President of the United States. Dixie is a badass freedom fighter, sent back from 2076 to kill him before he takes over the world and ruins the future. They hate each other. Then they accidentally fall in love.

Title: Your Boy
Logline: Home for the summer on Long Island, a shy black college student comes out to his oldest and closest friend. But after an internship in Manhattan leads him to an exhilarating gay social scene, the 21-year-old is caught between his newly confident lifestyle and the unpopular straight friend who once knew him best.

Title: American Tabloid
Logline: The true story of Generoso Pope, Jr., who with the help of the New York mob turned a small, local paper into the phenomenon that is The National Enquirer, laying the foundation for tabloid journalism as we know it today.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of Generoso Pope Jr. and the National Enquirer.

Title: Arc of Justice
Logline: Based on the book 'Arc of Justice: A Saga of Race, Civil Rights, and Murder in the Jazz Age' by Kevin Boyle. Chronicles the landmark civil rights trial of Dr. Ossian Sweet after he was charged with the murder of a white man.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on Kevin Boyle's book; the true story of Dr. Ossian Sweet.

Title: Brosio
Logline: Inspired by the work of artist John Brosio. When a man begins to lose all of the people close to him in a series of increasingly absurd natural disasters, he must find out why his world has been turned upside down.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Inspired by the art of John Brosio.

Title: Cancer Inc (f.k.a. Conundrum)
Logline: The true story of the corporatization of cancer in the United States told through the eyes of a British Wall Street analyst who uncovers the corruption behind the approval of a drug intended to treat prostate cancer.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: Come As You Are
Logline: An idealistic young woman's life begins to unravel when her job in social media exposes her to the darkest corners of humanity, sending her on a violent mission to take down not just the web's most vicious content, but its creators as well.

Title: Don't Be Evil
Logline: Google's Larry Page, Sergey Brin, and Eric Schmidt struggle with their corporate motto, 'Don't Be Evil,' in the face of their meteoric rise to a multi-billion dollar valuation and a major Chinese hacking incident.
Creator/Author/Director: Gabriel Diani & Etta Devine & Evan Bates
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. A satire featuring Google's founders.

Title: Dorothy & Alice
Logline: Dorothy Gale and Alice meet in a home for those having nightmares and embark on a journey to save the imaginations of the world.

Title: Escape
Logline: When a wrongly accused man is shipped to an Australian penal colony for five years, he quickly realizes his only chance of seeing his family again is to escape the prison with a gang of convicts and survive the deadly terrain that awaits on the outside.

Title: Escape from the North Pole
Logline: A young girl partners up with an elf, a Russian explorer and a reindeer to rescue Santa Claus from a band of evil elves and save the North Pole.

Title: The Expansion Project
Logline: A rookie Marine gets stranded on a hostile planet during humanity's space colonization with nothing but her exo-suit that's running out of fusion power.

Title: The Fifth Nixon
Logline: Watergate as experienced through the eyes of President Richard Nixon's personal secretary Rose Mary Woods.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Watergate through the eyes of Rose Mary Woods.

Title: Gadabout
Logline: In 1951, a manufacturing company stirs up curiosity when they publish a user's manual to a time machine called Gadabout TM-1050.

Title: George
Logline: The true story of the Reys, the husband and wife team who fell in love, created Curious George, and escaped the horrors of WWII in Europe together.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of H.A. and Margret Rey, creators of Curious George.

Title: The Great Nothing
Logline: A grieving thirteen-year-old girl hires a terminally ill, acerbic philosophy professor to prevent flunking the seventh grade. What begins as a homework assignment blossoms into an unlikely friendship and a new appreciation for life that neither will forget.

Title: Green Rush
Logline: A paroled ex-con agrees to help his daughter steal medical marijuana tax dollars from City Hall.

Title: The Grown Up
Logline: A con woman who pretends to read auras is hired by a wealthy woman to banish an evil spirit from her house, but it is soon clear that the fake exorcist is in over her head.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the short story 'The Grownup' by Gillian Flynn.

Title: Hack
Logline: Based on actual reports, a horrifying look inside the Democratic National Committee hack and the Russian manipulation of the 2016 election.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of the DNC hack and the 2016 election.

Title: Health and Wellness
Logline: A sociopath obsessed with self-improvement claws her way to the top of the fitness world, leaving a trail of broken bodies in her wake.

Title: Hughes
Logline: The story of writer/director John Hughes, whose emotionally honest high school movies helped to define American culture in the 1980s — but who, at the very height of his success, abruptly abandoned filmmaking for reasons that have never been fully explained.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the life of John Hughes.

Title: Innocent Monsters
Logline: A writer struggling to crack her second novel starts to lose her sense of reality as the book bleeds into her life...and her life bleeds back.

Title: Jellyfish Summer
Logline: A young black girl's family in 1960s Mississippi decides to harbor two human-looking refugees who have mysteriously fallen from the sky.

Title: Jihotties
Logline: In an effort to fund their start-up, two women catfish ISIS and get more than they bargained for when the CIA recruits them as spies.

Title: Keeper of the Diary
Logline: Chronicles Otto Frank's journey, with the help of a junior editor at Doubleday Press, to find a publisher for the diary his daughter Anne wrote during the Holocaust.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List — #4 overall. Based on the true story of Otto Frank publishing Anne Frank's diary.

Title: Key of Genius
Logline: The story of Derek Paravicini, a blind, severely autistic boy who needed an incredible teacher to help realize his world-class musical ability.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of Derek Paravicini.

Title: Kill Shelter
Logline: A darkly comic crime thriller concerning three groups of people dealing with blackmail gone wrong.

Title: The Kingbreaker
Logline: A CIA operative experienced in taking down kings and installing their replacements is brought in to take down a dictator he helped install a few years prior.

Title: Let Her Speak
Logline: The story of Senator Wendy Davis and her 24-hour filibuster to save 75% of abortion clinics in Texas.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List — #2 overall. Based on the true story of Senator Wendy Davis.

Title: Liberation
Logline: The story of Nancy Wake, the most decorated servicewoman in World War II, who led resistance fighters in a series of dangerous missions in Nazi-occupied France.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of Nancy Wake.

Title: Lionhunters
Logline: A rogue cop suffers a gunshot wound in 1987 and wakes from a coma thirty years later, where he is partnered with a mild-mannered progressive detective — his son.

Title: Little Boy
Logline: The true story of the man who dropped the world's first atomic bomb on Hiroshima and his unexpected journey back to ground zero.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: Mad, Bad, And Dangerous To Know
Logline: An underdog identical twin accidentally kills her too-perfect sister only to discover murder suits her as she becomes compulsively embroiled in the life of a mafia assassin.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the Chloe J. Esposito book trilogy.

Title: The Man from Tomorrow
Logline: The story of visionary entrepreneur Elon Musk, who after being ousted from PayPal, guides SpaceX through its turbulent early years while simultaneously building Tesla.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the life of Elon Musk.

Title: Meat
Logline: A misanthropic man notices bizarre changes in himself, his wife, and the animals inhabiting the territory around their homestead as they attempt to survive self-imposed isolation.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. | Also on ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25' (all-time; ~#11; rated 'impressive', as of Jul 2026). SS review carried a SendSpace script link (2017 — likely expired).

Title: MOXIE
Logline: To combat crime in near-future Los Angeles, the FBI creates supercops based on specific genetic sequences. To their shock, their best candidate is a vulgar stripper named Moxie.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. (Distinct from the 2021 Amy Poehler film 'Moxie.')

Title: Newsflash
Logline: On November 22nd, 1963, Walter Cronkite puts everything on the line to get the story right as a president is killed, a frightened nation weeps, and television comes of age.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of Walter Cronkite reporting the JFK assassination.

Title: On
Logline: In a slightly futuristic/hyper-efficient Manhattan, a newly-single book editor purchases a customizable sex android to assuage her broken heart. When her toy's closed feedback loop starts to alter her personality, she must reevaluate the merits of a perfectly-compatible partner.

Title: One Thousand Paper Cranes
Logline: The incredible story of Sadako Sasaki, a young girl living in Hiroshima when the atomic bomb was dropped. Years later when she gets leukemia, she hears about the legend that if someone folds one thousand paper cranes, a wish will be granted. At the same time, aspiring writer Eleanor Coerr learns of Sadako's story and becomes determined to bring her message of hope and peace to the world.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of Sadako Sasaki.

Title: Panopticon
Logline: A look at the criminal justice and private prison system, told from the perspectives of a new inmate, a correctional officer, and a Wall Street hotshot.

Title: The Poison Squad
Logline: Based on the true story of Harvey Wiley, an eccentric chemist who conducted the first experiment on human tolerance to poison, which catalyzed a movement resulting in the founding of the Food and Drug Administration.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of Harvey Wiley and the founding of the FDA.

Title: The Prospect
Logline: Michael Jordan uses a year as a baseball prospect to find himself after his father's death.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on Michael Jordan's year as a baseball prospect.

Title: Queen Elizabeth
Logline: An uptight, high-achieving, black post-grad who becomes (increasingly) irreverent and (slightly) destructive when she realizes that the life she's living is not the life she wants.

Title: Ruin
Logline: A nameless ex-Nazi captain must navigate the ruins of post-WWII Germany to atone for his crimes during the war by hunting down and killing the surviving members of his former SS death squad.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List — #1 overall (top-voted script of 2017).

Title: Ruthless
Logline: After she is diagnosed with terminal brain cancer, a former assassin must carry out one last assignment in order to ensure her daughter's future.

Title: Skyward
Logline: The true story of two families who attempt to escape over the Berlin Wall using a hot air balloon in 1979.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of the 1979 Berlin Wall balloon escape.

Title: Sleep Well Tonight
Logline: Behind the walls of a maximum security prison, a naïve teenage inmate and a rookie correctional officer are forced into a drug-smuggling operation, while a looming conflict between rival gang members threatens to boil over.

Title: Social Justice Warrior
Logline: When a liberal, white, college sophomore who knows exactly how to fix society accuses her equally liberal professor of hate speech, it throws the campus and both their lives into chaos as they wage war over the right way to stop discrimination.

Title: Strongman
Logline: Based on the confusing, sometimes offensive, borderline-insane memories of David Prowse, the irascible Englishman behind Darth Vader's mask.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the memories of David Prowse (Darth Vader).

Title: The Thing About Jellyfish
Logline: After her best friend drowns, a seventh-grade girl is convinced the true cause of the tragedy was a rare jellyfish sting. Retreating into a silent world of imagination, she crafts a plan to prove her theory.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the novel by Ali Benjamin.

Title: This is Jane
Logline: An ordinary group of women provide 11,000 safe, illegal abortions in Chicago from 1968 through 1973.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on Laura Kaplan's book about the Jane Collective.

Title: Trapline
Logline: A captive boy's lifestyle is upended when his abductor asks for his help kidnapping a second child.

Title: V.I.N.
Logline: As Alex Haley struggles to write the autobiography of Malcolm X, his editor at Playboy assigns him a new interview: George Lincoln Rockwell, head of the American Nazi Party.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of Alex Haley interviewing George Lincoln Rockwell.

Title: Valedictorian
Logline: An obsessive type-A student vows to secure the valedictorian title before school ends by any means necessary, even murder.

Title: When in Doubt, Seduce
Logline: The true story of the early relationship between Elaine May and Mike Nichols.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List — #11 overall. Based on the true story of Elaine May and Mike Nichols.

Title: When Lightning Strikes
Logline: The story of 25-year-old Joanne Rowling as she weathers first loves, unexpected pregnancies, lost jobs, and depression on her journey to create Harry Potter.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List — #6 overall. Based on the life of J.K. Rowling.

Title: Where I End
Logline: In a world where your life can be saved, uploaded to a computer, and restarted in the case of your untimely demise, a husband returns from the dead, suspecting his wife may have been involved in his death.

Title: The White Devils
Logline: Cassius raises his sons, Malcolm and Mandela, isolated and alone in the woods. They have never met another person in their entire lives. When they find a mysterious wounded white girl, June, alone and lost in their woods, prejudice, lies and love set them on a collision course with the real world that puts all their lives at risk.

Title: Wyler
Logline: With Hitler laying waste to Europe and the United States refusing to answer the call to war, Jewish filmmaker William Wyler risks his career to make MRS. MINIVER, the most effective propaganda film of all time.
Notes: On the 2017 Black List. Based on the true story of William Wyler making Mrs. Miniver.

Title: Allison Adams
Logline: A little girl goes missing in a small town, and the key to finding her resides with four disparate women who share nothing in common except for the young girl's name...Allison Adams.

Title: American Rebel
Logline: The true story of Deborah Sampson, a woman from Massachusetts who risked her life by disguising herself as a man and joining the Continental Army to help fight for independence during the Revolutionary War.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the true story of Deborah Sampson.

Title: Barbarian
Logline: Based on the true story of Boudicca, a queen and warrior of the British Celtic Iceni tribe who led an uprising against the occupying forces of the Roman Empire.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the true story of Boudicca. (Distinct from the 2022 horror film 'Barbarian.')

Title: Battle of Alcatraz
Logline: A plan to escape from Alcatraz prison turns into a battle and hostage crisis that pits armed prisoners against US Army troops for two full days.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the true story of the 1946 Battle of Alcatraz.

Title: Bitter Pill
Logline: When an out-of-work divorced mother stops taking the court-ordered medication that made her feel like a zombie, her brazenly immoral, fifteen-year-old imaginary friend appears to get her life back on track.

Title: Blonde Ambition
Logline: In 1980s New York, Madonna struggles to get her first album released while navigating fame, romance, and a music industry that views women as disposable.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List — #1 overall (top-voted script of 2016). Based on the life of Madonna.

Title: Boyfriend Material
Logline: In the near future, a heartbroken girl who hates technology participates in an experiment that allows you to program a robot into your dream man.

Title: The Builder
Logline: A look inside the tortuous drama of Donald Trump's first Manhattan real estate deal, his relationships with troubled older brother Freddy and famed fixer Roy Cohn, and how they all created the Trump of today.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the early career of Donald Trump and Roy Cohn.

Title: Burn Run
Logline: In war-torn Afghanistan, an American mercenary team is hired to escort a 20-ton heroin seizure bound for destruction but get caught up in a botched drug deal when their leader goes rogue, forcing them to overcome various enemies who wish to steal it.

Title: The Burning Season
Logline: A primatologist takes her teenage daughter to a remote region of Madagascar, where her determination to save endangered lemurs puts their relationship and safety at risk.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the short story by Laura Van Den Berg.

Title: Captain Infinity
Logline: When his comic book obsessed five-year-old brother is diagnosed with cancer, Oscar Ruiz attempts to shield him from the truth by convincing him that his 'treatment' is all part of a top secret program to become a superhero.

Title: Carnada
Logline: In post-Cuban Missile Crisis Miami, an ambitious young CIA agent receives a career-making opportunity in becoming the case officer for a charismatic Cuban ambassador offering to help eliminate Fidel Castro. As their relationship grows, however, he begins to question the mission and the true motives of his superiors.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on true events.

Title: Contingency Protocol
Logline: When futuristic technology renders the Federal Witness Protection Program obsolete, the U.S. Government begins using Time Travel to hide high-profile witnesses in the Past. When a security breach occurs, a U.S. Marshal and her witness struggle to find their way back to the Present Day while evading assassins.

Title: Dark Money
Logline: The true story of a young stock trader who goes to work for billionaire tycoon Steve Cohen and gets involved in the biggest insider trading scandal in history.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List — #9 overall. Based on the true story of the SAC Capital insider trading scandal.

Title: A Deconstruction of Reality
Logline: Tasked with finding a game changing take for the sixth Jason Bourne movie, Tom Milton goes deep down the rabbit hole of cracking the story. With the guidance (and abuse) of a professor from his past and Bourne himself, Tom begins workshopping scenes that begin to bleed into real life in unexpected ways.

Title: Donziger
Logline: After twenty years, Steven Donziger wins a $19 billion verdict against Chevron for poisoning rural lands in Ecuador. Chevron responds by hiring heavy hitter Randy Mastro determined to have the entire verdict thrown out.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the true story of Steven Donziger vs. Chevron.

Title: Escape From Sarajevo
Logline: Based on the author's father's escape from Sarajevo in 1992, the harrowing and heartbreaking tale of one man's mission to get back to his family.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the author's father's escape from Sarajevo in 1992.

Title: Fairy Godmother
Logline: When sought after Fairy Godmother Faye is hired by a mind-bogglingly gorgeous teenage client, Kenzie, to find her true love with the hottest prince in the land, Faye finds herself facing an unfamiliar challenge when the prince starts falling for her instead.

Title: The Fall
Logline: Finding themselves in the middle of an alien invasion, a newly divorced couple must survive a dangerous real-time journey on foot from downtown Atlanta to the suburbs, where their young children are home alone.

Title: Fruitcake
Logline: The remarkable true story of an unremarkable church-going accountant who stole $17 million in the biggest fruitcake heist of all time.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: The Gun Show
Logline: A famous top cop wakes up twenty years after a shootout and is reinstated to solve the case that killed his old partner. Only problem: he's a bull in a China shop, and 2016 is a very different time than the 1990s world he's used to. To teach him the ropes, he's paired with his old partner's son, a risk averse traffic cop.

Title: Hart You
Logline: Estranged siblings pose as a married couple in the Amalfi Coast to win a cash prize.

Title: Hummingbird
Logline: A black-ops assassin's latest mark forces her to question the truth of her identity and ultimately confront the unsettling fact that she's more than a hired gun, she's an entirely new breed of weapon.

Title: The Hunchback
Logline: Defying an isolationist, fear-mongering government, a gypsy revolutionary, a mutinous soldier, and a deaf hunchback become the champions of human tolerance and the symbols for a peaceful future in a dystopian revision of Victor Hugo's novel.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. A dystopian revision of Victor Hugo's novel.

Title: James Cameron's Titanic
Logline: A 'Rosencrantz and Guildenstern Are Dead' take on Titanic. An overprotective mother attempts to keep her teenage daughter, young son, and rowdy sister together while sailing on the doomed voyage of the Titanic in 1912.

Title: The King of L.A.
Logline: Set in the 1980s, based on the true story of a man who went from star DEA agent to convicted drug trafficker in the worst corruption scandal in DEA history.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: Kings Canyon
Logline: At the height of the Cold War, Air Force pilot Lieutenant David Steeves vanishes over the Sierra Nevada mountains in a high-value jet. Fifty-four days later, left for dead, he crawls out of the wild with an impossible tale of survival. The media and military brand him a hero, only to call him into question when discrepancies in his story point to his being a hoax, or worse — a traitor.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the true story of David Steeves.

Title: The Kings of Maine
Logline: Living with his wife and child in a trailer while working as a janitor, Stephen King struggles with alcoholism and his own dark history as he attempts to complete Carrie.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the early life of Stephen King.

Title: The Last Days of Night
Logline: Attorney Paul Cravath takes on Thomas Edison in a legal battle to determine whether Edison or his rival George Westinghouse will provide the electricity to light America.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on Graham Moore's novel.

Title: Layla...and Other Assorted Love Songs
Logline: Based on the lives and times of Eric Clapton, George Harrison, and Pattie Boyd and the tumultuous love triangle that inspired an entire generation of music.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the lives of Eric Clapton, George Harrison, and Pattie Boyd.

Title: Let the Evil Go West
Logline: In the 1800s, a father desperate to care for his family finds a barrel of gold, that may be less fortune than great curse.

Title: A Letter from Rose Kennedy
Logline: Told through a series of letters to family members, the tragic true story of Rosemary Kennedy, a vibrant, passionate young woman and oldest daughter of Joe and Rose Kennedy. Born with a severe learning disability, Rosemary so worried her father with her erratic behavior that he believed the stigma of mental illness in the family would ruin his plans to build a political dynasty. He hid her away in convents and sanitariums and ultimately had her lobotomized.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List — #10 overall. Based on the true story of Rosemary Kennedy.

Title: Liberty
Logline: The truly astonishing tale of Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi – the French sculptor wholly responsible for designing, building, and delivering the Statue of Liberty across the Atlantic to where it stands today.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the true story of Frédéric Auguste Bartholdi and the Statue of Liberty.

Title: Linda and Monica
Logline: The absolutely crazy true story of the relationship between Monica Lewinsky and Linda Tripp, the woman who nearly destroyed the Clinton presidency - and herself in the process.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List — #11 overall. Based on the true story of Monica Lewinsky and Linda Tripp.

Title: Man Alive
Logline: After an alien invasion takes over the minds of humankind, one survivor known as 'Man' must make peace with the alien force to fight a greater evil.
Creator/Author/Director: Joseph J. Greenberg & David Robert Mitchell

Title: Maximum King!
Logline: An outlandish, surreal imagining of how in 1985 Stephen King wrote and directed his horror classic MAXIMUM OVERDRIVE.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. A surreal imagining of Stephen King directing Maximum Overdrive.

Title: The Miserable Adventures of Burt Squire
Logline: The unbelievable true story of a family man from Alaska in the midst of a midlife crisis who embarks on what he hoped would be a dream sailing vacation but ends up shipwrecked in the Atlantic Ocean with a charming but psychotic sea captain who has decided to stop taking his medication.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List — #13 overall. Based on a true story.

Title: Mr. Toy
Logline: The true story of Marvin Glass, brilliant, charismatic, self-loathing, paranoid, demanding – and probably the greatest toy inventor of all time.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the true story of Marvin Glass.

Title: Northeast Kingdom
Logline: When Angie witnesses the betrayal and murder of her father during a smuggler's drop, the vigilant and determined young woman calls in the help of a mysterious female assassin to seek revenge against all those responsible.

Title: The Olympian
Logline: The true story of an underdog rower trying to make it into the 1984 Olympics, told through the story of his relationships with his coach, his father, his fiancée, and with competition itself.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List — #3 overall. Based on a true story.

Title: Orb
Logline: Inspired by actual events from a phenomenon that occurred in 1974, a young married couple discover a mysterious metallic orb in the woods near their house following a meteor shower. While the unusual object dazzles the couple by exhibiting some awe-inspiring properties, its true purposes are revealed to be far more sinister than they could ever imagine.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Inspired by actual events from a 1974 phenomenon.

Title: Reason of State
Logline: Twenty four hours inside the British Prime Minister's abode at 10 Downing Street.

Title: Revolver
Logline: Based on true events in Anchorage, Alaska in 1966, a teen girl attempts to sneak into the hotel where the Beatles are staying to lose her virginity to George Harrison.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List — #14 overall. Based on true events.

Title: Rugged
Logline: Four dysfunctional female coworkers get lost in the wilderness during a team-building trip and must work together in order to survive.

Title: Saving Charlie Chaplin
Logline: With Nazi agents attempting to stop the production of a film that is poking fun at Hitler, an FBI agent and an LAPD detective must team up to protect a drunk and angry Charlie Chaplin from being kidnapped.

Title: The Time Traveler's La Ronde
Logline: An exploration of relationships as a man witnesses different types of love across the ages.

Title: Turned On
Logline: When a socially awkward engineer creates an android to fill in for her, things go awry when it becomes self actualized.

Title: Two Butterflies
Logline: Torn apart by tragedy, two estranged sisters are forced to reunite and confront their differences after one sister must be transported to an Alzheimer's facility.

Title: Untitled Alexander McQueen Project
Logline: An emotionally charged depiction of the six months leading up to one of Alexander McQueen's greatest shows, probing both his personal and professional personas.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the life of Alexander McQueen.

Title: Untitled Fairy Tale Project
Logline: A handsome prince falls in love with a commoner, so her fairy Godmother turns her into a perfect princess. But when the spell works a little too well, the prince embarks on a quest to reverse it, not realizing that he's endangering the entire fairy tale universe.

Title: Untitled Lax Mandis Project
Logline: A frustrated film exec at odds with the state of his industry is forced to work with the one person who is making him question everything.

Title: Voyagers
Logline: The cosmic love story of Carl Sagan and Ann Druyan.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List — #5 overall. Based on the love story of Carl Sagan and Ann Druyan. (Distinct from the unrelated 2021 sci-fi film 'Voyagers.')

Title: The Woman with Red Hair
Logline: The extraordinary true story of Johanna 'Hannie' Schaft, a young woman who dropped out of college to join the Dutch Resistance during WWII, eventually becoming one of their most talented assassins and one of the Nazis' most wanted enemies.
Notes: On the 2016 Black List. Based on the true story of Hannie Schaft.

Title: 105 and Rising: The Fall of Saigon
Logline: Amid the chaos of a failing nation on April 29, 1975, the objective was clear: safely evacuate all remaining Americans from Saigon. But to Major Jim Kean and Ambassador Graham Martin, the moment represented something else: the final opportunity to uphold promises and obligations to the people of South Vietnam.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the true story of the fall of Saigon.

Title: An African Western
Logline: When a young African woman's village is destroyed by a 'Christian' paramilitary unit and her sister is kidnapped, she uses every weapon at her disposal on an odyssey to save her last living relative.

Title: Ares
Logline: The survival story of an astronaut whose space capsule crash lands in the African desert and whose mission to space was part of a larger conspiracy.

Title: Atlantic Wall
Logline: A lone American paratrooper stranded behind enemy lines hours before D-Day, is tasked with delivering intelligence critical to the outcome of the war and compelled to fulfill a promise to protect the young son of a murdered ally.

Title: Bare Knuckle
Logline: New York City 1862: The bare knuckle boxing champion, Bad Jack, develops a crush on a common French girl and uses his political influence to send her Irish lumberman husband off to the Civil War so he can take her for his own.

Title: Battle of New Orleans
Logline: In one of the greatest untold stories in American history, General and future President Andrew Jackson reluctantly partners with world-renowned pirate Jean Lafitte to lead a rag-tag team of soldiers against the indomitable British Army in the climactic battle of the War of 1812.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the true story of the Battle of New Orleans.

Title: BlackFriars
Logline: Set around the Blackfriars massacre of 1978, the true story of a Boston police officer and a con-man-turned-witness, who become unlikely friends hiding out in Bermuda, ultimately perpetrating a con on the Boston DA they're tasked to serve.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on a true story (the 1978 Blackfriars massacre).

Title: Boomtown
Logline: A slick corporate investigator with a closely guarded secret discovers a sinister criminal conspiracy in North Dakota oil boom country.

Title: Boy
Logline: A teenage boy is born with special abilities and spends his childhood switching names and cities in order to keep his identity hidden. When he loses control and accidentally kills his father, he and his mother have to go on the run.

Title: The Boy
Logline: Sixteen years after stabbing a classmate to appease a legendary phantom known as the Boy, a repentant woman returns to her hometown to live with her sister and nephew. But as the Boy continues to haunt her she must face her deepest terror and discover the truth about the Boy before he claims her nephew.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. (Distinct from the 2016 horror film 'The Boy.')

Title: Bubbles
Logline: A baby chimp is adopted by the pop star Michael Jackson. Narrating his own story, Bubbles the Chimp details his life within The King of Pop's inner circle through the scandals that later rocked Jackson's life and eventually led to Bubbles' release.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List — #1 overall (top-voted script of 2015). Narrated by Michael Jackson's chimpanzee Bubbles.

Title: Bump
Logline: After an accidental hit and run, a young lawyer's entire life unravels over the span of one night as he attempts to dispose of a corpse that turns out to be way more than an innocent victim.

Title: The Canyon
Logline: When parents reunite with their grown children for a road trip to hike the Grand Canyon, the family conflicts on the way prove far more grueling than the twenty mile hike.

Title: Carnival
Logline: A deadly carnival knife-thrower hunts down the members of a powerful crime syndicate who murdered his sister.

Title: Castle Drive
Logline: Based on real events, the story of the writing of Fatal Vision, the 1983 bestselling true crime classic that chronicles the summer journalist Joe McGinniss spent with 'Green Beret Killer' Jeffrey MacDonald while he was on trial for the brutal murder of his wife and children.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on real events (Joe McGinniss writing 'Fatal Vision' and the Jeffrey MacDonald case).

Title: Circle of Treason
Logline: Based on the book 'Circle of Treason: A CIA Account of Traitor Aldrich Ames and The Men He Betrayed' by Sandra Grimes and Jeanne Vertefeuille. When two women in the CIA suspect there is a mole working there, they have to overcome the institution's hierarchy and chauvinism to bring down the traitor.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the book about CIA traitor Aldrich Ames.

Title: Crimson Trail
Logline: Devastated by the cold-blooded murder of his family, a devout frontier preacher risks his soul to lead a posse in pursuit of the Harpe Brothers -- America's first serial killers.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on a true story (the Harpe Brothers).

Title: Crook County
Logline: A whistleblower's harrowing journey into an undercover FBI operation in 1980's Cook County, Illinois, sending him toe-to-toe with corrupt judges, hustling defense attorneys, and Chicago mobsters; and ultimately resulting in the largest number of convictions of government officials in the history of the United States.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the true story of Operation Greylord.

Title: Cut and Run
Logline: A female urologist and a retired hooker form an unlikely friendship when they team up to take down a notorious sex trafficker in Miami.

Title: Damsel
Logline: After first being locked up in a tower and then hidden deep in the forest to prevent a prince from rescuing her, long-haired Rapunzel vows to get revenge.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. (Distinct from the 2018 Zellner brothers film and the 2024 Netflix film titled 'Damsel.')

Title: Do No Harm
Logline: An ambitious surgeon's life takes a dangerous turn when she indulges in an affair with a doctor whose god complex challenges her own.

Title: Final Journey
Logline: A mistreated elderly Inuit woman is forced out of her village to survive alone on the savage arctic tundra.

Title: The Fisherman
Logline: A fisherman sails out of Martha's Vineyard in search of the shark that killed his fellow sailors while they were stranded in the water for four days after their ship was attacked by a Japanese submarine.

Title: Francis and the Godfather
Logline: Facing financial ruin, auteur filmmaker Francis Ford Coppola is forced to direct the adaptation of Mario Puzo's pulp novel The Godfather, pitting him against legendary mega-producer Robert Evans in this comedy about art versus commerce.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the making of The Godfather.

Title: Free Agent
Logline: A female NBA executive for the Golden State Warriors pursues the biggest free agent of her career while managing a messy divorce and a complicated relationship with a younger colleague and his teenage sister.

Title: Great Falls
Logline: After negligently killing a hunter with their patrol car, an alcoholic Sheriff's Deputy and her superior officer must decide what to do with the only witness to their crime - a death row inmate only days from execution.

Title: Green River Killer
Logline: Based on the Eisner Award winning graphic novel of the same name. The story follows the detectives in the Green River Killer case as they attempt to unravel not only the facts behind the gruesome series of murders that plagued the Pacific North West for over a decade, but also the psychosis of the killer they now find themselves face to face with.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the Eisner Award-winning graphic novel.

Title: Hammerspace
Logline: A terminally-ill teenager looking for answers about his missing father finds a key that unlocks an opening to an alternate animated dimension, and a new friend helps him repair his broken family.

Title: Homegrown
Logline: A teenage boy, raised in total seclusion and indoctrinated by his enigmatic father, is sent to live with a foster family and enrolled in a public high school when his father is incarcerated, threatening their relationship and causing the boy to question everything he's learned.

Title: I Believe in America
Logline: A behind-the-scenes look at the making of THE GODFATHER, revealing the creative forces behind one of the finest American films ever made were all as cunning and ruthless as the mobsters portrayed in Mario Puzo's bestseller.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Credited as Terence Clyne. A behind-the-scenes look at the making of The Godfather.

Title: Ida Tarbell
Logline: Ida Tarbell's magazine series 'The History of the Standard Oil Company,' would not only change the history of journalism, but also the fate of Rockefeller's empire, shaken by the powerful pen of its most implacable observer.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the true story of Ida Tarbell.

Title: Landslide
Logline: The story of America's search for terrorist Abu Musab al-Zarqawi, and the man who played a key role in tracking him down by using psychological warfare.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on Tony Camerino's book 'How to Break a Terrorist.'

Title: The Libertine
Logline: After the Head of the French National Assembly is placed under house arrest for accusations of sexual assault, he must live in a guarded apartment with his estranged wife until the case comes to a close.

Title: A Life Fantastic
Logline: A single mother with a terminal diagnosis embarks on a road trip with her eccentric nine-year-old son and nanny to find a loving and progressive family that will accept him for whoever he may grow up to be.

Title: Mayday 109
Logline: Based on actual events, a young John F. Kennedy struggles to save the crew of his PT Boat after it is sunk by a Japanese warship during World War II.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the true story of JFK and PT-109.

Title: Morningstar
Logline: The war is over. A bitter and uneasy truce has been reached with an invading alien race, and a new cold war has begun. Fueled by suspicions of an alien spy in their ranks, the United Nations Intelligence Division entrusts their top agent, Martin Webber, with finding the mole.

Title: Nyad
Logline: Based on the true story of marathon swimmer Diana Nyad, who in 2013, after 4 failed attempts and at the age of 64, became the first person ever to open-swim from Cuba to Florida (55 hours non-stop) overcoming impossible odds, personal tragedy, and 103 miles of open ocean.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the true story of Diana Nyad. (A separate Nyad film was produced by Netflix in 2023.)

Title: Our Week with Waller
Logline: After getting laid off, two Americans cash in their vacation pay and head abroad to Spain where their paths collide with one of the world's most dangerous terrorists.

Title: Pandemonium, Splendidly Managed
Logline: A local Phoenix newscaster at the pinnacle of local celebrity slowly descends into the depths of madness while trying to become a game show host in Los Angeles.

Title: Preconception
Logline: A couple on the verge of parenthood embarks on a pre-baby bucket list.

Title: Queen of the Air
Logline: Based on the book 'Queen of the Air' by Dean N. Jensen. The story of famed trapeze artist Lillian Leitzel and her relationship with fellow trapeze artist Alfredo Codona, who were two of the most famous entertainers of the world during their time.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the book by Dean N. Jensen.

Title: Reagan
Logline: When Ronald Reagan falls into dementia at the start of his second term, an ambitious intern is tasked with convincing the commander-in-chief that he is an actor playing the president in a movie.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List — #9 overall. (Distinct from the 2024 Reagan biopic.)

Title: Resurface
Logline: An underwater earthquake decimates a research crew working at the bottom of the Mariana Trench, leaving two survivors with limited resources to ascend 35,000 feet and reach the surface before their life support runs out.

Title: The Rocket
Logline: Roger 'The Rocket' Clemens, one of the greatest pitchers of all time, has 4672 strikeouts, 354 wins and a record 7 Cy Young awards. This is the story of why he is not in the Hall of Fame.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List — #4 overall. Based on the life of Roger Clemens.

Title: Salem Village
Logline: Fifteen years after the witch trials, the scars of Salem are reopened when young women begin showing up dead. Desperate for answers, the town elders turn to a man of science to uncover the truth behind the terrifying events plaguing their community.

Title: Septillion to One
Logline: A former FBI agent working the fraud unit of the Texas State lottery falls in love with a woman he's investigating after she mysteriously hits the jackpot three times.

Title: The Set Up
Logline: Two co-dependent female best friends' friendship is put through the wringer after a blind date gone awry sends them on a dangerous, all-in-one-night adventure with a drug dealer.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. (Distinct from the 2018 Netflix film 'Set It Up.')

Title: The Shave
Logline: A dirty cop, exonerated in the murder of a high school honor student, visits the boy's father at his barbershop and, while receiving a straight razor shave, listens to him recount the story of his son's life.

Title: Song of Treblinka
Logline: A renowned Jewish conductor plots a revolt with fellow prisoners as he attracts the respect of his Nazi captors in Poland's most notorious death camp.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: A Speck in the Sea
Logline: Based on the article 'A Speck in the Sea' by Paul Tough. The true story of the rescue of lobster fisherman John Aldridge who fell into the ocean in the middle of the night forty miles off Montauk with no life vest and no way to signal where he was.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the article by Paul Tough; true story of John Aldridge.

Title: Spring Offensive
Logline: Dr. Liz Scott, a British epidemiologist with the World Health Organization, is tasked with containing an outbreak of Ebola in Afghanistan's war torn Helmand province as the Taliban's assault on allied forces threatens to turn the localized outbreak into a global catastrophe.

Title: Star One
Logline: Two CIA agents must overcome their opposing worldviews to evade the Soviet and Chinese armies during a dangerous mission to Tibet in 1950. The true story of the first CIA agent ever to die in the line of duty; the 'first star' on the CIA Memorial Wall.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the true story of the first CIA officer killed in the line of duty.

Title: Tomorrow on the Runway
Logline: Kermit St. Lucy has a ridiculous name and is ridiculously ill-equipped to deal with adult responsibilities. After a tragedy shakes him to his essence, Kermit takes it upon himself to look after and raise two young children.

Title: Treasure Island
Logline: Based on the Robert Louis Stevenson classic, young Jim Hawkins finds a map to a buried fortune; to claim it means a perilous voyage, pirate treachery, and the wicked mentorship of Long John Silver.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the Robert Louis Stevenson novel.

Title: True Fan
Logline: After interfering with a foul ball during a Chicago Cubs playoff game, Steve Bartman was tortured and stalked by die hard Cub fans for potentially costing them their first National League pennant since 1945. Years later, in a new town with a new identity — but depressed, overweight and working a dead end job — Steve meets a woman who gives him a new lease on life and reason to live.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List — #7 overall. Based on the true story of Steve Bartman.

Title: Untitled Larry Hillblom Project
Logline: The strange-yet-true story of the battle over the estate of Larry Lee Hillblom, billionaire founder of DHL.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the true story of Larry Hillblom, founder of DHL.

Title: Untitled Sociopath Project
Logline: A successful New Yorker's seemingly perfect life starts unraveling once he's suspected for the murder of his co-worker, and his only alibi is the doppelganger that's begun stalking him.

Title: Verona
Logline: A re-imagining of Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. A reimagining of Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet.

Title: The Virginian
Logline: A young, down-and-out George Washington, desperate to join the British Army, accepts a dangerous mission to conquer a French fort and save the American colonies.
Notes: On the 2015 Black List. Based on the young George Washington.

Title: The Wretched Emily Derringer
Logline: Gleefully terrifying her small town as a serial killer known as 'The Misfit Butcher,' 13-year-old Emily Derringer becomes annoyed when a new killer comes to town and residents begin attributing his sloppy murders to the Misfit Butcher. In a macabre coming of age story, Emily must deal with her competition while also taking on the other trials and tribulations of junior high school life.

Title: Aether
Logline: In near future London, a revolutionary technology has been invented that can record sounds hours after they were made. Detective Harry Orwell, inventor of this technology, is part of a pilot program where investigators record and analyze past sound waves and finds himself the prime suspect while investigating a string of brutal murders.

Title: A Beautiful Game
Logline: A high school soccer star's personal life becomes complicated leading up to his championship game as he develops a relationship with his soccer coach.

Title: Beauty Pageant
Logline: After they unwittingly get their daughters disqualified from the child beauty circuit, two warring stage-mothers are forced to go head to head in an adult beauty pageant.

Title: Beef
Logline: The manager of a fast food chain in Muncie, Indiana gets in over his head with some bookies.

Title: Berliner
Logline: As the Berlin Wall is being constructed at the height of the Cold War, a veteran CIA agent searches for a Soviet mole who has already killed several fellow agents, including a young agent he's mentored.

Title: Bismarck
Logline: As Britain struggles through the darkest hours of World War II, a naval officer, raw from the loss of his ship during the evacuation of Dunkirk, is thrust into the thick of the hunt for the Nazi super-battleship, Bismarck.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: Black Winter
Logline: On the eve of a US-Soviet disarmament treaty, a British scientist and a NATO medical investigator discover a secret Soviet plot to unleash a terrifying biological weapon.

Title: Blink
Logline: Years after being fully paralyzed during an infamous bank robbery, a man is taken hostage for the secrets in his head. His only form of communicating with the outside world — and outsmarting his captors — is his ability to blink.

Title: Boston Strangler
Logline: In the 1960s, a determined detective puts his life and career on the line to solve the case of the Boston Strangler.
