Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on the true Boston Strangler case. (Distinct from the 2023 Hulu film.)

Title: The Bringing
Logline: A private investigator investigates a mysterious murder at a downtown Los Angeles hotel and uncovers its dark supernatural history.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on true events.

Title: Cartoon Girl
Logline: When a young boy finds out that the cartoon character he's in love with is based on a real girl, he drags his single father on a road trip to track her down.

Title: Cascade
Logline: An oil tanker collides with an Iranian patrol boat in the Strait of Hormuz, triggering a chain of tragic disastrous events.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List — #7 overall. Based on the film 'The Day Britain Stopped.'

Title: Catherine the Great
Logline: Sophia Augusta takes control of her life, her marriage, and her kingdom becoming Russia's most celebrated and beloved monarch: Catherine the Great.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List — #1 overall (top-voted script of 2014). Based on the life of Catherine the Great.

Title: Celeritas
Logline: When a missing astronaut crash lands forty years after he launched having not aged a day, his elderly twin brother helps him escape the NASA scientists hunting him. As the government closes in, neither brother is who they claim to be.

Title: The Defection
Logline: After the Edward Snowden affair, an intelligence contractor defects to North Korea, taking a mysterious bag with him, and the CIA hires an expert trained during the Cold War to help with the case.

Title: Dodge
Logline: A genre bending action comedy about a pill popping thug who begins to develop superpowers.

Title: Echo
Logline: A CIA drone coordinator battles his own psychological health while trying to decipher whether his wife has been replaced.

Title: The Eden Project
Logline: When a race of genetically modified humans living secretly among us declare war on Man, the fate of the world is in the hands of a rogue 'Synthetic' named Eve and a young girl who is about to discover she's not all human.

Title: Erin's Voice
Logline: A deaf computer genius' world is thrown into turmoil when he meets a troubled coffee shop waitress whose voice turns out to be the only thing he can miraculously hear.

Title: Everybody Wants Everything
Logline: As his life reaches its neurosis-inducing midpoint, a married man asks himself an eternal question with no real answer — 'Am I living the life I want to be living, or do I need to start over before it's too late?' Torn between two lives, he's forced to do the one thing he doesn't want to do — make a choice.

Title: Forgive Me
Logline: How Mike Wallace helped to create 60 Minutes and how years later, he confronted and dealt with his own depression.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on the life of Mike Wallace.

Title: A Garden at the End of the World
Logline: In a post-apocalyptic world, a recluse, trying to recreate trees to produce new life, takes in a young girl who is on the run from some bad men, including her father.

Title: I Am Ryan Reynolds
Logline: An inside look at the marriage, career, and mental state of 2010's Sexiest Man Alive.

Title: In Real Time
Logline: A young man convinced that his mentally unstable sister needs to relive her high school prom from ten years prior to overcome her depression goes to great lengths to recreate that event.

Title: In This, My Darkest Hour
Logline: A man rises to power during the California gold rush, tearing his family apart.

Title: Jackpot
Logline: After a group of bumbling teachers win a large amount of money, their greed and incompetence put them on a hilarious path toward death and destruction.

Title: The Long Haul
Logline: A self-destructive trucker estranged from his son travels cross country with a problematic nephew whom he barely knows.

Title: The Man in the Rockefeller Suit
Logline: The story of Clark Rockefeller, a con artist thought to be American royalty until he kidnapped his young daughter initiating a manhunt that revealed his true identity.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on the true story of Clark Rockefeller.

Title: Matriarch
Logline: A prison psychologist has 48 hours to convince a serial killer to tell her the location of her final victim before she is executed.

Title: Merc
Logline: When a disgraced former soldier finds success by working for a private security company, the illegal tactics the company employs challenges his worldview.

Title: Moonfall
Logline: The investigation of a murder on a moon colony.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. (Distinct from the unrelated 2022 Roland Emmerich film 'Moonfall.')

Title: The Munchkin
Logline: A little person private eye investigates the disappearance of a young actress in 1930s Hollywood, leading him to uncover conspiracies involving THE WIZARD OF OZ and Metro Goldwyn Mayer brass.

Title: North of Reno
Logline: A down and out prison guard attempts to murder a recently released inmate and steal a half million dollars in hidden heist money.

Title: One Fell Swoop
Logline: A self-centered divorce attorney's life takes an unexpected turn when he is guilted into spending time with the family of a one night stand who dies in a freak accident.

Title: Plus One
Logline: Just out of a long term relationship and realizing that all her friends have married, Rachel discovers that her only remaining wing-woman is Summer, a loud and oversharing wildcard.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. (Distinct from the 2019 rom-com 'Plus One.')

Title: Possession: A Love Story
Logline: In a seemingly perfect marriage, a man discovers that he is actually wedded to a demon inhabiting another woman's body.

Title: Professor Pasghetti
Logline: A famous children's author, with an affinity for drugs and hookers, finds himself on a journey of self-discovery with a dead stripper and her eight year old son.

Title: Road to Oz
Logline: The early days of brilliant, whimsical author L. Frank Baum, who gave the world The Wonderful Wizard of Oz.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on the life of L. Frank Baum.

Title: Rockingham
Logline: A look into the mania of the OJ Simpson trial, through the eyes of Simpson's sports agent Mike Gilbert and Los Angeles Police Department Detective Mark Fuhrman.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List — #2 overall. Based on the O.J. Simpson trial.

Title: Rothchild
Logline: A young, well-educated loner kills the members of his mother's estranged family one-by-one in hopes that he will inherit the family's vast fortune.

Title: The Search
Logline: An expert tracker battles his demons while on a journey to rescue his estranged older brother who has vanished in the uncharted wilderness of the Northwest.

Title: The Secret Ingredients of Rocket Cola
Logline: Twin brothers with opposite personalities are separated at a young age and go on to live drastically different lives, eventually being reunited in the effort to save the company 'Rocket Cola' despite their love of the same woman.

Title: Seducing Ingrid Bergman
Logline: Ingrid Bergman and war photographer Robert Capa engage in a passionate, life-changing romance in post-World War II Paris.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on Chris Greenhalgh's novel.

Title: Shadow Run
Logline: A viral attack puts lives in danger, forcing a CIA agent to initiate a secret prisoner exchange of Russia's most notorious spy for the American scientist who can create a cure.

Title: The Shower
Logline: At a baby shower for their longtime friend, the attendees suddenly find themselves in the middle of a different type of shower: meteors that release a vapor turning men into blood-hungry aliens.

Title: Situation Comedy
Logline: A young woman, feeling directionless, stumbles upon a mysterious courtyard where she is transported into a sitcom-like universe, becoming a major character on this 'TV show.'

Title: The Swimsuit Issue
Logline: A nerdy high schooler, who fancies himself an amateur photographer, attempts to create a 'Swimsuit Issue' featuring his high school classmates in hopes of raising enough money to go to summer camp.

Title: Syndrome (E)
Logline: A detective solving the case of a disturbing film with subliminal images that is killing people who come in contact with it discovers a greater evil.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on the novel 'Syndrome E' by Franck Thilliez.

Title: The Takeaway
Logline: A young, play-it-safe, art restorer is swept up in a whirlwind romance with her charming boss, who turns out to be a world-class thief.

Title: Uncle Shelby
Logline: The little-known personal, heartbreaking, and darker side of cartoonist/author Shel Silverstein.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. Based on the life of Shel Silverstein.

Title: The Wild Ones
Logline: In a corrupt Southern town, a dangerous sociopath runs bareknuckle boxing fights that pit its youths against each other.

Title: Wonka
Logline: A dark, reimagining of the Willy Wonka story beginning in World War II and culminating with his takeover of the chocolate factory.
Notes: On the 2014 Black List. (Distinct from the 2023 film 'Wonka.')

Title: Yellowstone Falls
Logline: After an apocalyptic event, a mother wolf is separated from her mate and the rest of the pack, and has to protect her cubs from swarms of mutated humans.

Title: 1969: A Space Odyssey of How Kubrick Learned to Stop Worrying and Land on the Moon
Logline: With NASA's Apollo program in trouble and the Soviets threatening nuclear war, a female PR operative conspires with NASA's Public Affairs Office to stage a fake moon landing in case Armstrong and Aldrin fail, the goal being to generate public excitement that will aid the U.S. in winning the Cold War. But the op is faced with the biggest challenge of all: filming the fake lunar landing with temperamental Stanley Kubrick.

Title: Beast
Logline: With the hope of starting over, a reformed criminal with an ultra-violent past returns home, but when he finds his own family leading his teenaged son down the same path of destruction, he will stop at nothing to save his child.

Title: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood
Logline: Set in 1950s Manhattan, Fred Rogers journeys from a naïve young man working for NBC to the host of the beloved children's PBS show, Mr. Rogers' Neighborhood.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the life of Fred Rogers. (Distinct from the 2019 film of the same title.)

Title: Beauty Queen
Logline: An unhappily married woman and her best friend go on a road trip to Las Vegas to compete in the Miss Married America competition.

Title: The Boy and His Tiger
Logline: The true story of Bill Watterson, the creator of Calvin & Hobbes.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the life of Bill Watterson, creator of Calvin & Hobbes.

Title: Broken Cove
Logline: After his brother is found brutally murdered, a man hellbent on revenge returns to his decrepit Irish fishing village home armed only with a mysterious list of names his brother left behind.

Title: Burn Site
Logline: After a young teenage girl is murdered, her stepfather falls back on his dark and violent past to find her killer.

Title: Bury the Lead
Logline: A desperate, attention hungry journalist concocts a story that ironically proves to be true and finds himself engulfed in a dangerous underworld of murder and mayhem.

Title: Capsule
Logline: A young man's life is turned upside down when he mysteriously begins to receive metallic capsules containing messages from his future self.

Title: The Civilian
Logline: After an American doctor has his identity stolen by a covert operative, he must assume the dangerous mission of the one who stole it in order to clear his name.

Title: Clarity
Logline: What if the world woke up tomorrow to scientific proof of the afterlife?

Title: The Company Man
Logline: Based on true events, the story of former CIA agent Edwin Wilson who went behind enemy lines to secure weapons contracts and report information back to the CIA shortly after the Cold War. Edwin had a meteoric rise in the agency until company policies changed and he was unceremoniously fired, but he continued to operate as a man without a country and became public enemy number one in the US Attorney's office.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the true story of CIA agent Edwin Wilson.

Title: Crown
Logline: In exchange for a lighter prison sentence, a young hacker goes undercover for the FBI in a sting operation to find and steal a super computer virus with the help of a team of unsuspecting hackers.

Title: Diablo Run
Logline: While on a road trip to Mexico, two best friends are forced to enter a thousand mile death race with no rules.

Title: Dig
Logline: After his villainous father-in-law kidnaps his daughters, Sol, a tough-as-nails mountain man, travels across the frigid Appalachian mountains seeking vengeance.

Title: Dogfight
Logline: A 15-year-old boy discovers that his kidnapped older brother has been living in a hidden, meth-producing compound, and infiltrates the camp in hopes of helping his brother escape.

Title: Elsewhere
Logline: After his girlfriend dies in a car accident, a man finds his true soulmate, only to wake from a coma and learn his perfect life was just a dream — one he is determined to make real.

Title: The Fixer
Logline: A man who works in wealth management, and also has his hands in a number of less than ethical enterprises, begins collaborating with a Los Angeles based drug dealer. The dealer just so happens to have the man's son as one of his runners in the drug fueled LA nightlife.

Title: Free Byrd
Logline: After being diagnosed with dementia, a retired 50-something stunt motorcyclist sets out to perform one last jump.

Title: Frisco
Logline: A forty-something pediatric allergist, who specializes in the hazelnut and is facing a divorce, learns lessons in living from a wise-beyond-her-years terminally ill 15 year old patient when she crashes his weekend trip to a conference in San Francisco.

Title: From Here to Albion
Logline: A tragic accident in a coastal English town sets off a chain of violence when a malevolent assassin attempts to punish all involved, including a dirty cop who is intent on covering up the truth.

Title: Fully Wrecked
Logline: An R-rated talking car from the 80s is brought back into service and teamed up with the son of his former partner, a befuddled cop looking to earn his stripes.

Title: Gay Kid and Fat Chick
Logline: Two high school misfits become costumed vigilantes and take out their frustrations on the students who have bullied them throughout high school.

Title: The Golden Record
Logline: The true story of how Carl Sagan fell in love while leading the wildest mission in NASA history: a golden record to encapsulate the experience of life on earth for advanced extraterrestrial life.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the true story of Carl Sagan and the Voyager Golden Record.

Title: Half Heard in the Stillness
Logline: A young man, who is inadvertently rescued after living ten years in the basement of the child predator who abducted him, struggles to reunite with his family, while the detective in charge of his case investigates the link between his discovery and the recent disappearance of another local boy.

Title: Ink and Bone
Logline: When a female book editor visits the home of a horror writer so he can complete his novel, she finds that all of his creations are holding him hostage.

Title: Inquest
Logline: After the death of Princess Diana, a reluctant investigator is hired to ascertain whether her death was premeditated. And in the process, he begins to uncover a conspiracy that compromises his own safety.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the death of Princess Diana.

Title: The Killing Floor
Logline: A war veteran slaughterhouse worker and his friend discover a small fortune in heroin hidden inside a processed cow and maneuver to hold onto their find and cash out to save his grandfather's house as the bad guys come looking for their wayward stash.

Title: Last Minute Maids
Logline: Two lovable losers run into trouble after they start a service cleaning up the stuff you don't want your loved ones to find once you die.

Title: Line of Duty
Logline: MACBETH meets THE DEPARTED in this modern retelling of Shakespeare's play, focusing on the tragic rise and fall of NYPD Officer Sean Stewart, a heroic narcotics detective pushed to the dark side of police corruption by his scheming wife and a well-timed prophecy.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. A modern retelling of Shakespeare's Macbeth.

Title: The Line
Logline: A corrupt border crossing agent must decide what is more important — saving his soul or inflating his bank account — when he discovers a young illegal boy who escaped a cartel hit on the border between the US and Mexico.

Title: Make a Wish
Logline: A 14-year-old boy with terminal cancer has one last wish — to lose his virginity — and convinces his reluctant football star Make-A-Wish partner to help him score.

Title: Man of Sorrow
Logline: Based on true events, the story centers on Oscar Wilde who goes from renowned playwright to losing everything personally and professionally.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the life of Oscar Wilde.

Title: The Mayor of Shark City
Logline: When a difficult film shoot spirals hopelessly out of control into a living nightmare, an ambitious young director must face his greatest fears to turn a troubled production into the biggest movie of all time. Set on Martha's Vineyard during the summer of 1974, this is the untold story of the making of JAWS.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the making of Jaws.

Title: Mississippi Mud
Logline: In the middle of major financial problems, a down on his luck southerner's life begins to unravel when he accidentally runs over and kills a runaway girl.

Title: Nicholas
Logline: With the Roman empire on the brink of collapse, a 4th century bishop takes up arms to lead the armies of Constantine the Great into battle against the ruthless emperor, changing the face of Rome and begetting one of the greatest legends in history.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the legend of Saint Nicholas.

Title: The Politician
Logline: A disgraced Governor and his underachieving accomplice go on the run from the FBI, US Marshals and a gang of hardened drug dealers.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. (Distinct from the 2019 Netflix series 'The Politician.')

Title: Pox Americana
Logline: In the Old West, a group of soldiers go on a mission to slaughter a peaceful tribe in retaliation for another tribe's attack on a white settlement, only to suffer at the hands of a devastating disease.

Title: Pure O
Logline: In order to overcome violent visions brought on by OCD, a promising young writer is urged by family and friends to step outside her comfort zone to experience the excitement and vulnerability of truly living.

Title: Queen of Hearts
Logline: Oxford instructor Charles Dodgson (aka Lewis Carroll) tells stories to the dean's daughter Alice, and her sisters, while falling in love with the dean's wife.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the life of Lewis Carroll.

Title: RANDLE Is Benign
Logline: Follows a woman in the 80's who works at an IBM-like company and is at the forefront of artificial intelligence research. When her project (named RANDLE) hits a major milestone indicating that she may have actually achieved AI, it is unexpectedly hijacked by the agenda of the company's mysterious CEO. As she dives deeper into the corporate agenda, she learns that there may be a connection between her project and the 1981 assassination attempt on Ronald Reagan.

Title: The Remains
Logline: Three former childhood friends with a complicated history get back together to spread the ashes of their friend who recently died.

Title: Revelations (a.k.a. Lord of Light)
Logline: A prison psychiatrist meets a death row inmate on the verge of his execution who claims to be the only thing stopping the end of the world. As she begins to investigate his predictions, she finds them to be eerily accurate, and that she may be a central figure in the events to come.

Title: Section 6
Logline: An exploration of the formation of Great Britain's secret intelligence agency, Military Intelligence, Section 6, known more commonly as MI6.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List — #2 overall. Based on the formation of MI6.

Title: Seed
Logline: After suffering a devastating miscarriage a young woman and her fiancé travel to Italy where she meets his family for the first time, but her grief turns to shock when the local doctor declares that she's still pregnant. And while her fiancé and his family seem delighted by the news, she begins to suspect their true motives are quite sinister.

Title: Sovereign
Logline: A man goes to space to destroy the ship that, upon going sentient, killed his wife.

Title: The Special Program
Logline: The true story of Jack Goldsmith, a young attorney who took charge of the White House's Office of Legal Counsel, then courageously took on Vice President Cheney and his powerful inner circle when he discovered they were running a number of illegal activities through their so-called Special Program.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List — #5 overall. Based on the true story of Jack Goldsmith.

Title: Sugar in My Veins
Logline: A 14-year-old female prodigy finds companionship for the first time when she befriends an older handsome man.

Title: Superbrat
Logline: Temperamental tennis champion John McEnroe is sucked into a dangerous and ludicrous law enforcement sting during Wimbledon in 1980.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on John McEnroe.

Title: Sweetheart
Logline: A young hit woman tries to escape the business but finds herself in more danger after a high school reunion and a one night stand.

Title: Tchaikovsky's Requiem
Logline: A conductor investigates the great composer's seemingly unnatural death and unlocks the mysteries of the man himself while preparing to debut Tchaikovsky's final symphony.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the life and death of Tchaikovsky.

Title: The Shark Is Not Working
Logline: When his big break finally arrives, an idealistic young movie director, Steven Spielberg, risks failing to complete the movie JAWS when his 25 foot mechanical shark stops working.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on the making of Jaws.

Title: Time & Temperature
Logline: TIME & TEMPERATURE chronicles how Dale Julin (a low-level Fresno affiliate morning-show host) stumbled upon the biggest story of his life — and though he had reached the mid-point of his career without ever being a 'real journalist' — risked his career, his safety and his marriage to uncover the truth that a small atomic bomb exploded in Central Valley California during the Korean War, a secret that had been hidden for decades.
Notes: On the 2013 Black List. Based on a true story.

Title: Where Angels Die
Logline: A street-tough white social worker in the slums of Detroit acts on a dangerous and violent personal vendetta when he protects a young girl and her mother from her recently incarcerated boyfriend.

Title: A Country of Strangers
Logline: Based on true events. Inspector Geoff Harper conducts a forty year search for the Beaumont Children, three siblings taken from an Australian beach in January of 1966.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List — #2 overall. Based on the true story of the Beaumont Children.

Title: Americatown
Logline: In a China-dominated near future, a former LAPD officer attempts to save his family from destitution in Los Angeles by working for a crime lord in the American ghetto within a thriving Hong Kong.

Title: The Ballad of Pablo Escobar (n.k.a. The Devil's Angel)
Logline: A telling of the last years of Pablo Escobar's life, the war he waged on the Colombian government, and his fight to recapture his son's love and respect.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on the last years of Pablo Escobar.

Title: Black Box
Logline: When Air Force One crashes, a journalist discovers a cover up after gaining access to the plane's black box data and must unravel the mystery.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. (Distinct from the 2020 Blumhouse film 'Black Box.')

Title: Bleeding Kansas
Logline: A runaway slave and a sheriff must journey to rescue the kidnapped daughter of a Gubernatorial candidate against the backdrop of Kansas' induction into statehood and whether or not it would be a slave or free state.

Title: Border Country
Logline: A veteran goes to war against a crew of corrupt cops intent on controlling the US / Mexico border.

Title: The Broken
Logline: In 1967 Oklahoma, a war vet/farmer investigates the suspicious death of his estranged son in the next county. When he discovers his son's brutal murder was a covered-up gay bashing, he goes on a one-man mission to take down the corrupt sheriff responsible.

Title: Clive
Logline: After an accident causes a successful CEO to lose both legs, he is forced to re-evaluate his life and identity.

Title: Conversion
Logline: A preacher's wife, grieving from the loss of her teenage son and struggling to hold her family together, forms an unlikely friendship with a young street hustler who helps her understand her lost son and survive alcoholic depression.

Title: The Devil and the Deep Blue Sea
Logline: During WWII, a fifteen year old German boy is sent to America to spy for the Fatherland by joining a politically-connected family as an English war orphan. Tension mounts when the boy gets the chance to assassinate President Roosevelt.

Title: Devils at Play
Logline: In the Soviet Union in 1937, a worker of the People's Commissariat for Internal Affairs finds a list of traitors, which he thinks is going to be his way out.

Title: The Disciple Program
Logline: A man begins an investigation into his wife's mysterious death, only to find that it goes much deeper than he imagined.

Title: Doppelgangers
Logline: Buttoned-down 20-something Eric isn't ready to marry his longtime girlfriend Abby. His only way out is through slacker Sam, his identical twin brother, who offers to pose as Eric to do the dumping. But the plan becomes a disaster when Sam realizes Abby is actually really cool — and falls head over heels for her.

Title: The Eel
Logline: An escaped convict is ensnared in a plot by a corrupt Sheriff to kidnap the young heiress to an oil fortune, complicating his quest for freedom.

Title: El Tigre
Logline: A family vacation goes horribly awry when the father is mistaken for the most ruthless drug lord in Mexico, El Tigre.

Title: Ex Boyfriend of the Bride
Logline: After discovering one of their high school sweethearts is getting married, two best friends head to the wedding in order to stop the bride from going through with it.

Title: The Final Broadcast
Logline: A man takes a job as a radio broadcaster in a small town, only to discover that it is embedded with a radical group planning mass destruction in conjunction with an upcoming lunar eclipse.

Title: From New York to Florida
Logline: An undisciplined boy is sent to Florida for the summer with his grandparents, and the drive south changes him forever.

Title: Fuck Marry Kill
Logline: Three best friends return for their high school reunion intent on righting all the wrongs done to them in high school, by either fucking, marrying, or killing their tormentors.

Title: George
Logline: When an acerbic alcoholic finds himself penniless and alone he has no choice but to crash his family's holiday. Years of alienating them makes for a stiff challenge, but eventually he subtly helps them heal.

Title: Glimmer
Logline: When three friends go missing on a camping trip in a forest rumored to be haunted, the two left behind discover clues that lead them to a safe deposit box containing video tapes showing exactly what happened to their friends.

Title: Goodbye, Felix Chester
Logline: After finding out he has a month left to live, high school junior Felix Chester focuses all of his time and energy on one goal: losing his virginity to his dream girl.

Title: Ground Control to Major Tom
Logline: After nine years, a NASA communications expert reconnects with the astronaut she believed to be dead and helps rescue him from space.

Title: Hey, Stella!
Logline: The story of how Marlon Brando won the role of Stanley Kowalski in Elia Kazan's Broadway play A Streetcar Named Desire.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on Marlon Brando and A Streetcar Named Desire.

Title: Hibernation
Logline: A wrongly convicted inmate volunteers for a hibernation experiment in exchange for one day of parole every five years, which he uses to prove his innocence and search for his missing daughter across an increasingly futuristic landscape.

Title: Hold on to Me
Logline: A ruthless and money-hungry woman uses a hapless man as a pawn in her criminal schemes.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on an article by Hillel Levin and Jim Keene.

Title: The Hooverville Dead
Logline: Set in St. Louis in the 1920s, washed up baseball player Will Cosgrove is a private eye with his older brother Ross. When Ross goes missing, Will stumbles upon a deep secret that the disease taking over the town is not quite what it seems and a mob boss turned Governor will do anything to keep the town's secret from being revealed.

Title: If They Move... Kill 'Em
Logline: After losing his luster and respect in Hollywood, famed director Sam Peckinpah hopes to direct his next great film with financial backing from Colombian drug lords and brings along a novice screenwriter to write the film in Colombia.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on director Sam Peckinpah.

Title: The Killing Spree
Logline: Heartbroken after being dumped by his longtime girlfriend, a guy's best friends devise the perfect plan for his recovery and teach him how to sleep with as many women as humanly possible.

Title: King of Heists
Logline: An unassuming man in elite New York society assembles a crew that pulls off the largest bank heist in American history in 1878.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on the book by J. North Conway.

Title: The Lighthouse
Logline: William Van Alen's building of the Chrysler Building and the competition to be the first to complete the world's tallest building.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on the building of the Chrysler Building. (Distinct from the 2019 Robert Eggers film 'The Lighthouse.')

Title: Man of Tomorrow
Logline: In an alternate 1940s reality, the US Government makes a deal with an indestructible gangster to kill Hitler in exchange for the city of Chicago, which he will build into his own utopia. Unfortunately his model city never comes to fruition and both he and his Bureau liaison get much of the slack for destroying one of America's greatest cities and now the government wants him dead.

Title: McCarthy
Logline: It is 1951. Junior Senator Joe McCarthy feels lost. He's anonymous, just another suit on Capitol Hill. He craves attention and celebrity. Inspired by the response of the American public to the House Un-American Activities Committee, McCarthy decides that communism will be his defining issue. What follows is nearly unthinkable — national fame, a shocking conspiracy, a sitting President afraid to oppose him, and a torrid journey of self destruction and paranoia.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on the life of Senator Joseph McCarthy.

Title: Midnight at Noon
Logline: On the run after robbing a bank during the great depression, two brothers find themselves trapped in the harsh region known as the Dust Bowl where a ruthless killer hunts them down.

Title: Monsoon
Logline: A dangerous love affair between a photojournalist and a black market smuggler set against the June 2001 massacre of the Royal Family in Nepal.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Set against the 2001 Nepalese royal massacre.

Title: Murder City
Logline: An ensemble crime story set in Detroit about an ex-con who goes back for one last heist in order to settle his family's debt.

Title: The One That Got Away
Logline: A twenty eight year old woman is about to get engaged to her great boyfriend, when the ex she hasn't gotten over moves back into town. All of a sudden she isn't sure if her boyfriend is really the one, and so she spends time decorating her ex's new condo, trying to figure it all out.

Title: Out of State
Logline: While driving his regular interstate bus route, an emotionally fractured ex-convict finds himself acting as a father figure to a forsaken young boy from the Philadelphia ghetto, even though he knows that the boy is smuggling drugs.

Title: The Paper Man
Logline: The true story of Matthias Sindelar, the Austrian footballer voted as the Sportsman of the Century and killed by Hitler's Nazi party.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on the true story of footballer Matthias Sindelar.

Title: Penny Dreadful
Logline: Desperate to hang on to his pregnant girlfriend, bumbling Dennis gets caught up in a kidnapping scheme gone awry, leaving him saddled with a sociopathic little girl who seemingly calls the shots.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. (Distinct from the Showtime series 'Penny Dreadful.')

Title: The Portland Condition
Logline: Set against the backdrop of rainy Portland, Oregon, a young man finds himself falling in love for the first time — only to receive a letter from his future self, warning him of impending heartbreak.

Title: Rodham
Logline: During the height of the Watergate scandal, rising star Hillary Rodham is the youngest lawyer chosen for the House Judiciary Committee to Impeach Nixon, but she soon finds herself forced to choose between a destined path to the White House and her unresolved feelings for Bill Clinton, her former boyfriend who now teaches law in Arkansas.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List — #4 overall. Based on Hillary Rodham Clinton.

Title: Seuss
Logline: As a young man, Ted Geisel meets his future wife Helen, who encourages his fanciful drawings, and in the 1950s when Ted is struggling professionally, Helen helps inspire the children's book that will become his first big hit, 'The Cat in the Hat.'
Notes: On the 2012 Black List — #3 overall. Based on the life of Dr. Seuss.

Title: Somacell
Logline: A female prison guard in the future, where prisoners are rehabilitated with virtual reality, discovers a conspiracy that puts her loyalty into question.

Title: Times Square
Logline: Set amidst the transformation of Times Square from New York's seediest neighborhood to the commercialized Disneyland it is today, when a secret from his past is unearthed, a young man's loyalties are divided between his neighborhood boss who raised him and the grizzled ex-cop who swore to protect him.

Title: Titans of Park Row
Logline: Young William Randolph Hearst goes to war against an aging Joseph Pulitzer as each tries to monopolize coverage of a mysterious homicide capturing New York's imagination, birthing the modern concept of sensationalist media coverage.
Notes: On the 2012 Black List. Based on William Randolph Hearst and Joseph Pulitzer.

Title: Untitled Blake McCormick Cop Comedy
Logline: Following a costume party where they dressed as cops, two best friends are mistaken for actual police officers and find themselves on the run, after being forced to bring a dangerous criminal back to the station.

Title: Whalemen
Logline: The leader of a fourteenth century Scottish whaling village must seek out and do battle with a whale many times larger than any he has ever seen in order to ransom back his son from the occupying English.

Title: Who Framed Tommy Callahan
Logline: An elementary school student searches for the truth behind the candy bar ring conspiracy that got his brother expelled.

Title: The Winter Kills
Logline: A disgraced cop pursues the serial killer who murdered his partner ten years ago, has resurfaced, and is killing again.

Title: Wunderkind
Logline: A Mossad employed father and his CIA agent son team up to hunt an escaped Nazi.

Two stories from 1974 are linked together — the unsolved murder of an LAPD officer and the nationally televised shootout in South Central Los Angeles between the Symbionese Liberation Army and the LAPD. The events are seen through the eyes of a pair of police partners, one black and one white.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. Based on true 1974 Los Angeles events.

Title: Bethlehem
Logline: A group of people struggling to survive a zombie apocalypse make an alliance with a vampire, trading themselves as food in exchange for protection since zombies don't eat vampires.

Title: Beyond the Pale
Logline: Teenage siblings suspect they've been ripped off by the town undertaker, but what they discover is much more sinister than either imagined.

Title: The Big Stone Grid
Logline: A cop is pulled into an underworld organization that brutally murders people to extort money out of others.

Title: Blood Mountain
Logline: After his team is ambushed and killed in Pakistan, a young army ranger must escort the world's most wanted terrorist over dangerous terrain in order to bring him to justice. While being hunted by both of their enemies, they must find a way to work together in order to survive.

Title: Breyton Ave.
Logline: A group of teens living without adults and under their own social order in a small fenced-in neighborhood are forced to face what they fear is the inevitable physical danger beyond the fence.

Title: Bridges on the Fort Point Channel
Logline: An Irish family in the 1970s, dealing with the loss of their father and the busing of black kids into white neighborhoods, decides to blow up all the bridges in Boston.

Title: Chewie
Logline: A satirical behind the scenes look at the making of Star Wars through the eyes of Peter Mayhew who played Chewbacca.

Title: Cities of Refuge
Logline: A former FBI psychologist is called in to investigate when a young girl goes missing after the apparent murder of her father and brother by two strangers in a small Oklahoma town.

Title: Crazy for the Storm
Logline: The true story of Norman Ollestad's relationship with his father, who thrust the boy into the world of extreme surfing and competitive downhill skiing at the age of three. But it was that experience that allowed an 11-year old Norman to survive a plane crash amidst a blizzard in the San Gabriel mountains.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. Based on Norman Ollestad's memoir.

Title: Cristo
Logline: A man is unlawfully sentenced to an infamous prison and escapes, then transforms himself into the mysterious Cristo and systematically destroys the men who manipulated and enslaved him.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. A modern reimagining of The Count of Monte Cristo.

Title: Dead of Winter
Logline: A teenage girl heads to a remote cabin in the mountains with her father and new stepmother — an experience the father hopes will bond the two ladies. But when a mysterious wounded Park Ranger shows up, family bonding will be the least of their concerns.

Title: Desperate Hours
Logline: A small town crippled by WWI and the Spanish flu finds itself facing major moral questions and a brutal invading force when a young girl shows up on a rancher's doorstep covered in blood.

Title: El Fuego Caliente
Logline: A remake of SOAPDISH: a desperate telenovela star dreaming of Hollywood stardom has her life implode, making her real life crazier than the insane show she made famous.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. A remake of Soapdish.

Title: The End
Logline: Four people — a veteran broadcaster in London, a sixteen year old girl and her boyfriend in Ann Arbor, and a devoted family man in Shanghai — each try to make peace with their lives before an interstellar event ends the world in six hours.

Title: Ezekiel Moss
Logline: A mysterious stranger who possibly has the power to channel the souls of the dead changes the lives of everyone in a small Nebraska town, especially a young widow and her 11-year-old son.

Title: Father Daughter Time: A Tale of Armed Robbery and Eskimo Kisses
Logline: A man goes on a three state crime spree with an accomplice, his eleven year old daughter.

Title: The Flamingo Thief
Logline: Grief stricken over his wife leaving him, a man finds solace in an odd activity: swiping figurines of flamingos.

Title: Flashback
Logline: A former NASA pilot with amnesia — also the first person to travel the speed of light — realizes he has the ability to travel back in time and along the way rediscovers his love for his wife.

Title: Friend of Bill
Logline: After a humiliating episode in New York, a young woman returns to her hometown and tries to deal with her alcoholism.

Title: Gaslight
Logline: Secretly imprisoned in a London insane asylum, the infamous Jack the Ripper helps Scotland Yard investigators solve a series of grisly murders whose victims all share one thing in common: dual puncture wounds to the neck.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. (Distinct from the 1944 film 'Gaslight.')

Title: Grim Night
Logline: A family has to defend themselves from the Grims, strange creatures who attack Earth and kill thousands one night every year.

Title: The Gun Eaters
Logline: Four hardened New York detectives race to apprehend a relentless spree-killer who's executing victims from Queens to Southampton in the span of a single day.

Title: Guys Night
Logline: Sick of brunches, bosses, and light beer, four co-workers set out on the mother of all guys nights in an attempt to rediscover their manhood.

Title: He's Fuckin' Perfect (n.k.a. The Social Life)
Logline: A social media savvy girl who is pessimistic about love finds the perfect guy and decides to use her internet research skills to turn herself into his perfect match.

Title: Home By Christmas: Bob Hope in Korea, 1950
Logline: Young Larry Gelbart goes on tour with his idol Bob Hope in the middle of the Korean War and learns the true price of patriotism.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. Based on Larry Gelbart and Bob Hope.

Title: How to Disappear Completely
Logline: A child prodigy tries to take control of his life away from his demanding parents.

Title: Hyperdrive
Logline: When a tough cop recruits a geeky sci-fi author to help him track down a mysterious murder witness, they find themselves in the middle of a space opera playing out here on Earth.

Title: In the Event of a Moon Disaster
Logline: An alternate telling of the historic Apollo 11 mission to land on the moon that examines what might have happened if the astronauts had crash landed there.

Title: Jurassic Park
Logline: A high school couple and two of their friends ditch school to catch a special preview screening of Jurassic Park.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. A coming-of-age comedy, unrelated to the Jurassic Park film franchise.

Title: The Knoll
Logline: A rookie cop and his potential flame witness JFK gunned down from the grassy knoll on November 22, 1963. Within hours, they're on the run from the murderers who desperately need them silenced.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. Set around the JFK assassination.

Title: The Last Drop (f.k.a. Sobered Up)
Logline: A fully functioning alcoholic meets the girl of his dreams and soon discovers that there's a lot more at stake than love if he doesn't clean up his act.

Title: The Last Witness
Logline: An FBI Agent interrogates an amnesiac, sole survivor of a Boston bombing in order to prevent future terrorist attacks.

Title: Leaving Pete
Logline: A recently divorced author is stunned when his ex writes a popular book about their breakup, and he has to keep that fact secret from his new girlfriend, who works for the book's publisher.

Title: Line of Sight
Logline: After a military coup takes out the executive branch of government, the country's survival depends on a Navy Seal sniper extraction team getting the Speaker of the House from Washington DC to New York.

Title: Little White Corvette
Logline: A down and out brother and sister go to Miami to sell a duffel bag of cocaine that they found in the trunk of a corvette left them by their dead father.

Title: Murders & Acquisitions
Logline: The world of high-stakes finance collides with that of high-priced hitmen when an ousted CEO decides to hire an assassin to kill the corporate raider who stole his company.

Title: On a Clear Day
Logline: When a powerful and mysterious force invades an American city, a young father must traverse the battle-torn city in an effort to save his wounded wife and rescue their stranded children. In the process, our hero becomes the target of an enemy who will stop at nothing to kill him.

Title: Pinocchio
Logline: A wooden puppet, Pinocchio, dreams of becoming a real boy. (Live-action retelling.)
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. A live-action retelling.

Title: Powell
Logline: Based on the true story of Colin Powell questioning the Bush administration leading up to his United Nations presentation where he made the case for going to war with Iraq.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. Based on the true story of Colin Powell.

Title: The Slackfi Project
Logline: A hapless and broken hearted barista is visited by two bad-ass soldiers from the future who tell him mankind is doomed, and he alone can save them.

Title: Subject Zero
Logline: A Frankenstein-like tale of a scientist who develops a powerful new drug that brings his son back to life after he dies in a terrible car accident. Unfortunately, the desperate experiment of a loving father leads to the creation of a flesh-eating zombie epidemic with horrific consequences.

Title: The Three Misfortunes of Geppetto
Logline: A prequel to the story of Pinocchio in which Geppetto endures a life of misfortune, war, and adventure, all to be with Julia Moon, his true love.
Notes: On the 2011 Black List. A prequel to Pinocchio.

Title: Untitled Hlavin Heist
Logline: An American thief living in Paris is coerced into pulling off a complex heist in order to save his kidnapped wife.

Title: Watch Roger Do His Thing
Logline: A retired hitman gets roped back into his old trade in order to save his friend's life and quickly finds himself caught in a struggle trying to finish the job, and get his family out of Chicago alive at the same time.

Title: The Wedding (n.k.a. The Wedding Guests)
Logline: A group of couples deal with their respective issues as they attend a wedding.

Title: Desperate Hours
Logline: A town crippled by WWI and the Spanish flu faces grave moral questions and a brutal invading force when a blood-covered young girl appears on a rancher's doorstep.
Notes: Source: ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25' (currently #1). Rating: GENIUS — one of only ~2 'genius' verdicts Reeves has ever given. Period spec set up at Johnny Depp's production company; dormant/unmade for years. No downloadable script hosted on the review — link field points to the review page.

Title: The Brigands of Rattleborge
Logline: A gang of bandits uses the cover of a torrential thunderstorm to rob and terrorize a small frontier town, sending a grief-stricken sheriff and a mysterious vengeful doctor after them.
Notes: Source: ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25'. Rating: impressive. #2 on the 2006 Black List; optioned by Warner Bros and famously never made — the classic 'great unproduced script.' Writer is now est. director (Bone Tomahawk, Dragged Across Concrete). No downloadable script hosted on the review — link field points to the review page.

Title: Vivien Hasn't Been Herself Lately
Logline: A young husband refuses to abandon his wife after she becomes violently possessed, enduring escalating horror to try to save their marriage.
Notes: Source: ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25'. Rating: impressive. Early Brian Duffield spec (No One Will Save You, Spontaneous, Babysitter). Possession-as-failing-marriage horror — strong tonal fit for the Evil Building / possession lane. No downloadable script hosted on the review — link field points to the review page.

Title: Street Rat Allie Punches Her Ticket
Logline: In a decaying futuristic domed city, a teenage skateboarding 'rat' who finally earns a ticket out must choose between escape and the younger crew who depend on her.
Notes: Source: ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25' (amateur discovery). Rating: impressive. 2019 Academy Nicholl Fellowship winner. No downloadable script hosted on the review — link field points to the review page.

Title: Cop Cam
Logline: Told entirely through a police body camera, a retiring cop's final shift spirals into a violent, escalating nightmare after a routine traffic stop goes wrong.
Notes: Source: ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25' (amateur discovery, rare amateur 'impressive'). Written by a serving Biloxi PD lieutenant — unrepped. Found-footage cop thriller that flirts with horror. High scouting value (net-new, cheap to option). Review may host a link; verify.

Title: The Disciple Program
Logline: A man investigating his wife's suspicious death uncovers a lethal conspiracy and becomes the target of elite killers.
Notes: Source: ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25' (its most famous amateur discovery). Rating: impressive. Unrepped writer discovered via SS in 2012; sold to WME with Mark Wahlberg / Morten Tyldum attached historically — still never made. No downloadable script hosted on the review — link field points to the review page.

Title: Your Bridesmaid Is a Bitch
Logline: A heartbroken man agrees to groomsman duties at his sister's wedding, only to discover the ex who shattered him is in the bridal party.
Notes: Source: ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25'. On the 2010 Black List (pre-dates the 2011-2025 8FLiX scrape, so net-new). Brian Duffield's breakout heartbreak comedy. No downloadable script hosted on the review — link field points to the review page.

Title: The Ballad of the Ember Knight
Logline: Amid 14th-century France's war with England, a mysterious man on the run is forced to team with a ragtag band of misfits who may hold the key to restoring the kingdom.
Notes: Source: ScriptShadow — Carson Reeves' 'My Top 25' (amateur discovery added May 2026). Rating: impressive. 186-page period/adventure spec by unrepped brothers Kieran & Brennan O'Dea. LIVE downloadable script — Source Link is the Google Drive PDF Reeves posted with the writers' permission. Review: https://scriptshadow.net/screenplay-review-the-ballad-of-the-ember-knight/

Title: Baddy
Logline: A cash-strapped LA sex worker facing eviction lifts the phone and ID of a wealthy client — not knowing he's a sadistic killer who will invade her home and hold her and her young daughter hostage to get them back.
Notes: Sourced from Black List website (blcklst.com) hosted scripts; PDF in hand (98 pp, no draft date on title page). Writer contact: meyongue@gmail.com. Blcklst script pages are login-gated — check your account for a newer uploaded draft. Mother-daughter survival thriller; killer escapes at the end (sequel/franchise hook).

Title: Bloody Show
Logline: A hard-drinking, recklessly self-destructive woman in the final days of her pregnancy holes up at a remote lake — where she must survive a serial killer, and her own labor, over one very bloody night.
Notes: Sourced from Black List website (blcklst.com) hosted scripts; PDF in hand (94 pp). Writer contact: betzerhere@gmail.com / 410-274-3868. Blcklst pages are login-gated — verify newer draft in your account. Title is the obstetric term; pregnancy slasher with a transgressive antihero lead (Day Ripper villain). Betzer is an indie filmmaker.

Title: Christmas Time Is Here
Logline: The true story of A Charlie Brown Christmas: in 1965, struggling producer Lee Mendelson, animator Bill Melendez, and Charles Schulz defy a doubting network — jazz score, real kids' voices, Bible verse and all — to make TV's most unlikely Christmas classic.
Notes: Sourced from Black List website (blcklst.com) hosted scripts; PDF in hand (111 pp, title page dated Jan 19, 2021). Writer contact: t.m.hayes@mac.com / 781-858-5977. Blcklst pages are login-gated — verify newer draft in your account. Warm biographical dramedy; NOTE: depends entirely on Peanuts life rights / IP (Schulz estate, WildBrain/Apple relationship) — rights status is the whole ballgame here.

Title: Couples Hang
Logline: When an impossibly perfect couple moves into their Chicago cul-de-sac, a suburban private eye and his best friend can't resist digging into the newcomers' past — and drag both families into the dangerous world their new friends have been running from.
Notes: Sourced from Black List website (blcklst.com) hosted scripts; PDF in hand (96 pp). Writer contact: shaunhittle@gmail.com. Blcklst pages are login-gated — verify newer draft in your account. Suburban couples-friendship comedy with a witness-protection/crime engine; opens on a torture flash-forward. Comps in the 'The 'Burbs / Keeping Up with the Joneses / Game Night lane.

Title: Cutting Numbers
Logline: A lonely clerk at the clandestine office that schedules every human death — one typed invoice number at a time — discovers her estranged father's number is about to come up, and must decide whether to break the bureau's one unbreakable rule.
Notes: Sourced from Black List website (blcklst.com) hosted scripts; PDF in hand (111 pp, 'Current Revisions: 7.15.24'). Writer contact: movonancken@gmail.com / 914-772-1126, Brooklyn NY. Blcklst pages are login-gated — verify newer draft in your account. Melancholy high-concept death-bureaucracy fantasy (dominoes motif); tonal comps: Beetlejuice afterlife-office, A Ghost Story, Eternal Sunshine adjacency.

Title: Heartbreakers
